op_000058.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` is equal to the intersection of all `ℂ`-submodules `W ≤ V` such that `bc.vacuum ∈ W` and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W`.
op_000058.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) = sInf {W : Submodule ℂ V | bc.vacuum ∈ W ∧ ∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W}
op_000058.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the intersection as `sInf` of the set of submodules, matching the existing local scratch theorem shape.

op_000058.proof_sketch: Use the existing theorem that any vacuum-containing creation-stable submodule contains the Fock span to get the `≤ sInf` direction by `le_sInf`. For the reverse inequality, show the Fock span itself is in the defining set: it contains the vacuum by `ket_zero`, and its `aDagger`-stability follows from the previously verified ladder-invariance theorem.
op_000058.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc
refine le_antisymm (le_sInf ?_) (sInf_le ?_)
· intro W hW
  exact AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_fock_span_le_of_vacuum_creation_stable_000039 (bc := bc) W hW.1 hW.2
· show bc.vacuum ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ∧
    ∀ v : V, v ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) →
      bc.aDagger v ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)
  constructor
  · simpa [bc.ket_zero] using
      (Submodule.subset_span (s := Set.range bc.ket) ⟨0, rfl⟩ :
        bc.ket 0 ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket))
  · intro v hv
    exact (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_fock_span_invariant_under_ladder_000010 (bc := bc) hv).2

op_000062.source_statement: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ) (v : V), bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) (bc.a v) + (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) v
op_000062.formalized_statement: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ) (v : V), bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) (bc.a v) + (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) v
op_000062.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly with Polynomial.aeval evaluated at the endomorphism bc.aDagger.

op_000062.proof_sketch: This target has a standard proof direction: it is the polynomial commutator identity `[bc.a, p(bc.aDagger)] = (p') (bc.aDagger)`. A clean route is polynomial induction on `p` using `Polynomial.induction_on'`, with the constant case immediate because `aeval` of a constant is a scalar multiple of `1`, hence commutes with `bc.a`. The additive step is routine from linearity of `bc.a`, `Polynomial.aeval`, and `Polynomial.derivative`. For the multiplicative `X * q` step, apply `bc.ccr_apply` to `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) v` to get the key rewrite `bc.a (bc.aDagger w) = bc.aDagger (bc.a w) + w`, then insert the induction hypothesis for `q`. The remaining polynomial identity is exactly `Polynomial.derivative_mul` together with `Polynomial.derivative_X`, and `Polynomial.aeval` being an algebra hom (`map_add`, `map_mul`, `Polynomial.aeval_X`). So this looks genuinely provable in Mathlib style, not a counterexample candidate.

op_000050.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V`, if `W ≠ ⊥`, `∀ v ∈ W, bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v ∈ W, bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, then `W` is not finite-dimensional over `ℂ`.
op_000050.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (W : Submodule ℂ V), W ≠ ⊥ → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W) → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W) → ¬ FiniteDimensional ℂ W
op_000050.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized in the repository's existing style for invariant submodules and finite-dimensionality.

op_000050.proof_sketch: Restrict `a` and `aDagger` to endomorphisms of `W` using the invariance hypotheses. Their commutator is still the identity on `W` by pointwise CCR. If `W` were finite-dimensional, taking `LinearMap.trace ℂ W` would force `0 = trace 1 = finrank ℂ W`, but `W ≠ ⊥` gives `Nontrivial W`, hence `(finrank ℂ W : ℂ) ≠ 0`, contradiction.
op_000050.proof_text:
intro hW hA hAD hfin
letI : FiniteDimensional ℂ W := hfin
let A : Module.End ℂ W :=
  LinearMap.codRestrict W (bc.a.domRestrict W) (by
    intro w
    exact hA w w.2)
let B : Module.End ℂ W :=
  LinearMap.codRestrict W (bc.aDagger.domRestrict W) (by
    intro w
    exact hAD w w.2)
have hccr : A * B - B * A = 1 := by
  ext w
  apply Subtype.ext
  dsimp [A, B]
  change bc.a (bc.aDagger (w : V)) - bc.aDagger (bc.a (w : V)) = (w : V)
  simpa using bc.ccr_apply (w : V)
have htrace := congrArg (LinearMap.trace ℂ W) hccr
rw [map_sub, LinearMap.trace_mul_comm, sub_self, LinearMap.trace_one] at htrace
haveI : Nontrivial W := Submodule.nontrivial_iff_ne_bot.mpr hW
have hne : (Module.finrank ℂ W : ℂ) ≠ 0 :=
  Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (Nat.ne_of_gt (Module.finrank_pos (R := ℂ) (M := W)))
exact hne htrace.symm

op_000060.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, evaluating `p` at `bc.aDagger` on the vacuum is equal to the polynomial support-sum `p.sum (fun n z => z • bc.ket n)`.
op_000060.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum = p.sum (fun n z => z • bc.ket n)
op_000060.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized using `Polynomial.aeval` and the canonical `Polynomial.sum` support-sum form from the statement.

op_000060.proof_sketch: Apply Mathlib's `Polynomial.aeval_endomorphism` to the endomorphism `bc.aDagger`, the vector `bc.vacuum`, and the polynomial `p`. The resulting sum is `p.sum (fun n z => z • (bc.aDagger ^ n) bc.vacuum)`, which is exactly `p.sum (fun n z => z • bc.ket n)` by the definition of `BosonCore.ket`.
op_000060.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:10:61: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `bc.aDagger` | AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:10:72: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `bc.vacuum` | AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:10:82: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `p`

op_000060.proof_sketch: The previous attempt failed only because the outer universal binders were not introduced. After introducing `V`, the instances, `bc`, and `p`, apply `Polynomial.aeval_endomorphism` to `bc.aDagger`, `bc.vacuum`, and `p`, then simplify with `BosonCore.ket`.
op_000060.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc p
simpa [BosonCore.ket] using (Polynomial.aeval_endomorphism bc.aDagger bc.vacuum p)

op_000065.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. Then there exists a bosonic core `bcS : BosonCore S` such that for every `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V` with `bc.vacuum ∈ W`, `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, there exists `bcW : BosonCore W` for which the inclusion map `S ↪ W` sends `bcS.vacuum` to `bcW.vacuum` and commutes with both restricted operators `a` and `aDagger`.
op_000065.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∃ bcS : BosonCore S, ∀ (W : Submodule ℂ V), bc.vacuum ∈ W → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W) → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W) → ∃ (hSW : S ≤ W) (bcW : BosonCore W), (Submodule.inclusion hSW) bcS.vacuum = bcW.vacuum ∧ (∀ s : S, (Submodule.inclusion hSW) (bcS.a s) = bcW.a ((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s)) ∧ (∀ s : S, (Submodule.inclusion hSW) (bcS.aDagger s) = bcW.aDagger ((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s))
op_000065.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the universal property using an explicit witness hSW : S ≤ W so the inclusion map Submodule.inclusion hSW is well-typed.

op_000065.proof_sketch: Use the Fock-span invariance theorem to show S is stable under both ladder operators and contains the vacuum, then apply the invariant-submodule inheritance theorem to get bcS. For any W satisfying the hypotheses, the Fock-span inclusion theorem gives hSW : S ≤ W, and the inheritance theorem gives bcW. The vacuum and operator compatibility equalities follow by subtype extensionality and the coercion equalities returned by the inheritance theorem.
op_000065.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc
let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)
change ∃ bcS : BosonCore S, ∀ (W : Submodule ℂ V), bc.vacuum ∈ W → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W) → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W) → ∃ (hSW : S ≤ W) (bcW : BosonCore W), (Submodule.inclusion hSW) bcS.vacuum = bcW.vacuum ∧ (∀ s : S, (Submodule.inclusion hSW) (bcS.a s) = bcW.a ((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s)) ∧ (∀ s : S, (Submodule.inclusion hSW) (bcS.aDagger s) = bcW.aDagger ((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s))
have hvacS : bc.vacuum ∈ S := by
  simpa [S, bc.ket_zero] using
    (Submodule.subset_span (s := Set.range bc.ket) ⟨0, rfl⟩ :
      bc.ket 0 ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket))
have haS : ∀ v : V, v ∈ S → bc.a v ∈ S := by
  intro v hv
  exact (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_fock_span_invariant_under_ladder_000010 (bc := bc) hv).1
have haDaggerS : ∀ v : V, v ∈ S → bc.aDagger v ∈ S := by
  intro v hv
  exact (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_fock_span_invariant_under_ladder_000010 (bc := bc) hv).2
rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_invariant_submodule_inherits_boson_core_000051
    (bc := bc) S hvacS haS haDaggerS with ⟨bcS, hbcS_vac, hbcS_a, hbcS_aDagger⟩
refine ⟨bcS, ?_⟩
intro W hvacW haW haDaggerW
have hSW : S ≤ W :=
  AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_fock_span_le_of_vacuum_creation_stable_000039 (bc := bc) W hvacW haDaggerW
rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_invariant_submodule_inherits_boson_core_000051
    (bc := bc) W hvacW haW haDaggerW with ⟨bcW, hbcW_vac, hbcW_a, hbcW_aDagger⟩
refine ⟨hSW, bcW, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
· apply Subtype.ext
  calc
    (((Submodule.inclusion hSW) bcS.vacuum : W) : V) = (bcS.vacuum : V) := rfl
    _ = bc.vacuum := hbcS_vac
    _ = (bcW.vacuum : V) := hbcW_vac.symm
· intro s
  apply Subtype.ext
  calc
    (((Submodule.inclusion hSW) (bcS.a s) : W) : V) = (bcS.a s : V) := rfl
    _ = bc.a (s : V) := hbcS_a s
    _ = bc.a (((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s : W) : V) := rfl
    _ = (bcW.a ((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s) : V) := (hbcW_a ((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s)).symm
· intro s
  apply Subtype.ext
  calc
    (((Submodule.inclusion hSW) (bcS.aDagger s) : W) : V) = (bcS.aDagger s : V) := rfl
    _ = bc.aDagger (s : V) := hbcS_aDagger s
    _ = bc.aDagger (((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s : W) : V) := rfl
    _ = (bcW.aDagger ((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s) : V) := (hbcW_aDagger ((Submodule.inclusion hSW) s)).symm

op_000067.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, then there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` such that for every `n : ℕ`, `Φ` sends the element of `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` represented by `bc.ket n` to `Finsupp.single n 1`.
op_000067.formalized_statement: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → ∃ Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ), ∀ n : ℕ, Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)
op_000067.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the represented ket as the canonical element of the span subtype using `Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)`.

op_000067.proof_sketch: Use the verified theorem that the ket family is linearly independent when the vacuum is nonzero. Then `Basis.span` gives a basis of `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` indexed by `ℕ`, and its `repr` map is the required linear equivalence. The image of each ket basis vector is `Finsupp.single n 1` by `Basis.repr_self`.
op_000067.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc hvac
refine ⟨(Basis.span
  (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006
    (bc := bc) hvac)).repr, ?_⟩
intro n
simpa using
  (Basis.repr_self
    (b := Basis.span
      (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006
        (bc := bc) hvac))
    (i := n))
op_000067.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:11:11: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `Basis.span` | AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:16:5: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `Basis.repr_self`

op_000067.proof_sketch: Use the verified linear independence of the ket family when the vacuum is nonzero. The canonical equivalence `LinearIndependent.linearCombinationEquiv` identifies finitely supported coefficient functions with the span of that family, so its inverse gives the required map from the span to `ℕ →₀ ℂ`. The image of each basis ket is `Finsupp.single n 1` by `LinearIndependent.repr_eq_single`.
op_000067.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc hvac
have hli : LinearIndependent ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) :=
  AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006 (bc := bc) hvac
refine ⟨hli.linearCombinationEquiv.symm, ?_⟩
intro n
simpa [LinearIndependent.repr] using
  hli.repr_eq_single n ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ rfl

op_000068.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, then there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` such that for every `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, `Φ` sends the restricted creation operator applied to `Φ.symm l` to the finitely supported function `m ↦ if m = 0 then 0 else l (m - 1)`, and `Φ` sends the restricted annihilation operator applied to `Φ.symm l` to the finitely supported function `m ↦ ((m + 1 : ℂ) * l (m + 1))`.
op_000068.formalized_statement: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → ∃ (Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)) (h_create : ∀ x : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), bc.aDagger (x : V) ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)) (h_annihilate : ∀ x : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), bc.a (x : V) ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)), (∀ (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (m : ℕ), Φ ⟨bc.aDagger (Φ.symm l : V), h_create (Φ.symm l)⟩ m = if m = 0 then 0 else l (m - 1)) ∧ ∀ (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (m : ℕ), Φ ⟨bc.a (Φ.symm l : V), h_annihilate (Φ.symm l)⟩ m = ((m + 1 : ℂ) * l (m + 1))
op_000068.statement_formalization_notes: Restricted creation and annihilation were encoded via explicit closure witnesses on the ket span, and the Finsupp images were specified coefficientwise.

op_000068.proof_sketch: The intended proof direction is plausible, but the provided sketch depends on `thm_ket_span_linear_equiv_finsupp_000067` and `thm_fock_span_invariant_under_ladder_000010`, which are not available in the checked local context, and the crucial step extending the ladder formulas from basis kets to arbitrary `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ` is not specified in a Lean-ready way. Without those exact APIs or a separate formal proof of span invariance and the `Finsupp` linear equivalence, I cannot produce reliable tactic code for `stmt`.

op_000068.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, then there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` such that for every `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, `Φ` sends the restricted creation operator applied to `Φ.symm l` to the finitely supported function `m ↦ if m = 0 then 0 else l (m - 1)`, and `Φ` sends the restricted annihilation operator applied to `Φ.symm l` to the finitely supported function `m ↦ ((m + 1 : ℂ) * l (m + 1))`.
op_000068.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∃ (h_create : ∀ v : V, v ∈ S → bc.aDagger v ∈ S) (h_annihilate : ∀ v : V, v ∈ S → bc.a v ∈ S) (Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)), (∀ l : ℕ →₀ ℂ, ∀ m : ℕ, Φ ⟨bc.aDagger (Φ.symm l : V), h_create (Φ.symm l : V) (Φ.symm l).property⟩ m = if m = 0 then 0 else l (m - 1)) ∧ ∀ l : ℕ →₀ ℂ, ∀ m : ℕ, Φ ⟨bc.a (Φ.symm l : V), h_annihilate (Φ.symm l : V) (Φ.symm l).property⟩ m = (((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℂ) * l (m + 1))
op_000068.statement_formalization_notes: Uses explicit invariant-subspace witnesses for the restricted operators and states the Finsupp formulas pointwise.

op_000068.proof_sketch: This target has a strong proof direction from existing results in `Derived.lean`. Let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`, obtain `h_create` and `h_annihilate` from `thm_fock_span_invariant_under_ladder_000010`, and obtain `Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` from `thm_ket_span_linear_equiv_finsupp_000067`; this is the standard Mathlib `LinearIndependent.linearCombinationEquiv` basis equivalence, with `Φ ⟨bc.ket n, _⟩ = Finsupp.single n 1`. Since `Φ.symm` is the linear combination map on the ket basis, it is enough to verify the creation/annihilation formulas on basis vectors `Finsupp.single n 1`. Those basis computations are exactly `bc.aDagger_ket`, `bc.a_ket_succ`, and `bc.vacuum_annihilate` for the `n = 0` annihilation case. Then linearity of `bc.aDagger`, `bc.a`, `Φ`, and `Φ.symm` extends the basis identities to arbitrary `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, yielding the stated pointwise formulas.

op_000071.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number * (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) - (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) * bc.number = Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p)
op_000071.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number * (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) - (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) * bc.number = Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p)
op_000071.statement_formalization_notes: This matches the existing repository notation for `bc.number` and `Polynomial.aeval`.

op_000071.proof_sketch: This has a standard proof pattern: first reduce to monomials, because both sides are ℂ-linear in the polynomial `p`. For `p = X^n`, the left side is the commutator `[bc.number, bc.aDagger ^ n]`, which should be exactly the existing derived power-commutator theorem `thm_comm_number_a_dagger_pow_000002`. The right side becomes `aeval bc.aDagger (X * derivative (X^n)) = aeval bc.aDagger ((n : ℂ) • X^n)`, and by the polynomial derivative formula this is `(n : ℂ) • bc.aDagger ^ n`. So the target should follow by matching the monomial case and extending by linearity of `Polynomial.aeval` and of the commutator expression.

op_000071.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number * (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) - (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) * bc.number = Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p)
op_000071.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number * (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) - (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) * bc.number = Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p)
op_000071.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition using existing BosonCore and Polynomial.aeval notation.

op_000071.proof_sketch: This looks like a standard polynomial-functional-calculus commutator statement, and the monomial basis gives a clean proof. For `p = X^n`, `Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p = bc.aDagger ^ n`, so the left side is exactly `[bc.number, bc.aDagger ^ n]`, which matches the derived power-commutator theorem `thm_comm_number_a_dagger_pow_000002` and yields `(n : ℂ) • bc.aDagger ^ n`. On the right, the standard derivative formula gives `X * derivative (X^n) = (n : ℂ) • X^n`, hence `Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (X * derivative (X^n)) = (n : ℂ) • bc.aDagger ^ n`. Since both sides are `ℂ`-linear in `p` and polynomials are spanned by monomials, the general statement should follow by linearity and `simpa` on the monomial case. So the target has a promising proof direction using the existing derived theorem rather than redoing the commutator induction from scratch.

op_000072.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (v : V) (μ : ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number v = μ • v → bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p + Polynomial.C μ * p)) v
op_000072.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (v : V) (μ : ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number v = μ • v → bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p + Polynomial.C μ * p)) v
op_000072.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement already matches the repository's existing `BosonCore` and `Polynomial.aeval` notation, so I kept it unchanged.

op_000072.proof_sketch: Use polynomial induction on p. The constant and add cases follow from linearity of aeval and bc.number. In the monomial-successor case p = C a * X^(n+1), rewrite aeval explicitly and apply thm_a_dagger_pow_shifts_eigenvalue_000005 to (bc.aDagger^(n+1)) v; the polynomial side matches by derivative_C_mul_X_pow and aeval_mul.
op_000072.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc v μ p hv
induction p using Polynomial.induction_on with
| C a =>
    calc
      bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.C a)) v)
          = a • bc.number v := by
              simp [Polynomial.aeval_add, Polynomial.aeval_mul, Polynomial.aeval_C,
                Module.End.mul_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
      _ = a • (μ • v) := by
            rw [hv]
      _ = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger
            (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative (Polynomial.C a) +
              Polynomial.C μ * Polynomial.C a)) v := by
            simp [Polynomial.aeval_add, Polynomial.aeval_mul, Polynomial.aeval_C,
              Module.End.mul_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply,
              smul_smul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
| add p q hp hq =>
    calc
      bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (p + q)) v)
          = bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v + (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) v) := by
              simp [Polynomial.aeval_add]
      _ = bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) + bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) v) := by
            rw [map_add]
      _ = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p + Polynomial.C μ * p)) v +
            (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative q + Polynomial.C μ * q)) v := by
            rw [hp, hq]
      _ = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger
            (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative (p + q) + Polynomial.C μ * (p + q))) v := by
            simp [Polynomial.derivative_add, Polynomial.aeval_add, mul_add,
              add_assoc, add_left_comm, add_comm]
| monomial n a hp =>
    have hshift :=
      AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_a_dagger_pow_shifts_eigenvalue_000005
        (bc := bc) (v := v) (μ := μ) (n + 1) hv
    calc
      bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.C a * Polynomial.X ^ (n + 1))) v)
          = a • bc.number ((bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1)) v) := by
              simp [Polynomial.aeval_mul, Polynomial.aeval_C, Polynomial.aeval_X_pow,
                Module.End.mul_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
      _ = a • ((μ + (n + 1 : ℂ)) • ((bc.aDagger ^ (n + 1)) v)) := by
            rw [hshift]
      _ = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger
            (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative (Polynomial.C a * Polynomial.X ^ (n + 1)) +
              Polynomial.C μ * (Polynomial.C a * Polynomial.X ^ (n + 1)))) v := by
            simp [Polynomial.derivative_C_mul_X_pow, Polynomial.aeval_add,
              Polynomial.aeval_mul, Polynomial.aeval_C, Polynomial.aeval_X,
              Polynomial.aeval_X_pow, Module.End.mul_apply,
              Module.algebraMap_end_apply, pow_succ', add_smul, smul_smul,
              mul_add, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm,
              add_assoc, add_left_comm, add_comm]

op_000072.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (v : V) (μ : ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number v = μ • v → bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p + Polynomial.C μ * p)) v
op_000072.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (v : V) (μ : ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.number v = μ • v → bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) v) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * Polynomial.derivative p + Polynomial.C μ * p)) v
op_000072.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition and matches existing repository notation for `BosonCore.number` and `Polynomial.aeval`.

op_000072.proof_sketch: This target has a standard proof by polynomial induction, and the key library/repo fact is the derived eigenvalue-shift theorem for powers of `aDagger` (`thm_a_dagger_pow_shifts_eigenvalue_000005`). For a monomial `p = C a * X^n`, `Polynomial.aeval` gives `a • (bc.aDagger ^ n) v`, and the derived theorem rewrites `bc.number ((bc.aDagger ^ n) v)` as `(μ + n) • (bc.aDagger ^ n) v`. On the right-hand side, `Polynomial.derivative` of `C a * X^n` is the usual `a * n * X^(n-1)`, so `X * derivative p + C μ * p` evaluates to exactly the same scalar combination `(n + μ)` times `(bc.aDagger ^ n)`. The `C` and `add` cases should then follow from `aeval` linearity together with linearity of `bc.number` (`map_add`, `map_smul`/central scalar action). So the direction is clearly proof, not counterexample; the remaining work is Lean-side normalization of scalar/`aeval` identities rather than new mathematics.

op_000044.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (m : ℕ), m ∈ l.support → (∀ n : ℕ, n ∈ l.support → n ≤ m) → (bc.a ^ m) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = ((((m.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * l m) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum
op_000044.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (m : ℕ), m ∈ l.support → (∀ n : ℕ, n ∈ l.support → n ≤ m) → (bc.a ^ m) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = (((m.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * l m) • bc.vacuum
op_000044.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition and matches existing repository notation.

op_000044.proof_sketch: This looks provable by a direct finite-support expansion, not by a counterexample. Since `bc.a ^ m` is a linear endomorphism, expand `Finsupp.linearCombination` as a finite sum and push `(bc.a ^ m)` through termwise using the standard `Finsupp.linearCombination_apply`/sum linearity facts. The key auxiliary lemma is `(bc.a ^ k) (bc.ket k) = ((k.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum`, proved by induction from `bc.a_ket_succ`; each step multiplies by the next scalar and uses `Nat.factorial_succ`. A second auxiliary lemma is that if `n < m` then `(bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket n) = 0`, by writing `m = n + r + 1`, first lowering `ket n` to a scalar multiple of `vacuum`, then applying one more `a` and using `bc.vacuum_annihilate`. With the hypothesis `∀ n ∈ l.support, n ≤ m`, every support term is either `n = m` or `n < m`, so all terms vanish except the `m`-term, leaving `(((m.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * l m) • bc.vacuum` after rearranging scalar multiplication.

op_000075.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, a vector `v : V` lies in `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` if and only if there exists a polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ` such that `v = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`.
op_000075.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (v : V), v ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ↔ ∃ p : Polynomial ℂ, v = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum
op_000075.statement_formalization_notes: Uses the existing `bc.ket` notation and the repository's `Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p` style.

op_000075.proof_sketch: Rewrite span membership using `Finsupp.mem_span_range_iff_exists_finsupp`, choose the polynomial `Polynomial.ofFinsupp c`, and convert between polynomial evaluation on the vacuum and the corresponding finite ket sum via `thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060`.
op_000075.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc v
constructor
· intro hv
  rcases Finsupp.mem_span_range_iff_exists_finsupp.mp hv with ⟨c, rfl⟩
  refine ⟨Polynomial.ofFinsupp c, ?_⟩
  simpa [Polynomial.sum_def, Polynomial.support_ofFinsupp, Polynomial.coeff_ofFinsupp,
    Finsupp.sum] using
    (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060
      (bc := bc) (Polynomial.ofFinsupp c)).symm
· rintro ⟨p, rfl⟩
  rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060 (bc := bc) p]
  refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
  intro n hn
  exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n))

op_000076.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, then every `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V` satisfying `W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`, `W ≠ ⊥`, `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W` is equal to `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`.
op_000076.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (W : Submodule ℂ V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) → W ≠ ⊥ → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W) → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W) → W = Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)
op_000076.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized conservatively as a universal statement over ambient submodules with explicit invariance hypotheses for `a` and `aDagger`.

op_000076.proof_sketch: This looks provable by the standard Fock-basis irreducibility argument. Using `thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006`, the vectors `bc.ket n` are linearly independent when `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, so every nonzero `w ∈ W` has a finite expansion in the ket basis with at least one nonzero coefficient. Because `W` is stable under both `bc.a` and `bc.aDagger`, it is also stable under `bc.number = bc.aDagger * bc.a`; on the ket span, `bc.number` acts diagonally by `thm_number_ket_eigenvalue_000001`. For a chosen nonzero `w`, polynomial interpolation in `bc.number` on the finite set of eigenvalues appearing in its support isolates some individual `bc.ket n ∈ W`. Then repeated use of `a_ket_succ` gives `bc.vacuum = bc.ket 0 ∈ W`, and repeated use of `aDagger_ket` gives every `bc.ket n ∈ W`. Since `W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` already, this yields equality.

op_000076.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, then every `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V` satisfying `W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`, `W ≠ ⊥`, `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W` is equal to `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`.
op_000076.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → ∀ (W : Submodule ℂ V), W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) → W ≠ ⊥ → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W) → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W) → W = Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)
op_000076.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly as irreducibility of the ket span under simultaneous a- and aDagger-invariant submodules.

op_000076.proof_sketch: Pick a nonzero vector w in W. Since W is contained in the ket span and is stable under a and aDagger, it is also stable under the number operator. Apply thm_number_polynomial_isolates_ket_000103 to w, then use number-stability to show some ket n lies in W. Repeatedly apply a and divide by the nonzero scalars n, n-1, ..., 1 to descend to the vacuum. Once the vacuum is in W, thm_fock_span_le_of_vacuum_creation_stable_000039 and the given aDagger-stability imply the full ket span lies in W, so equality follows.
op_000076.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc hvac W hWspan hWne hWa hWaD
rcases (Submodule.ne_bot_iff W).1 hWne with ⟨w, hwW, hw0⟩
have hnum : W ≤ W.comap bc.number := by
  intro v hv
  show bc.number v ∈ W
  simpa [BosonCore.number] using hWaD (bc.a v) (hWa v hv)
rcases
    AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_number_polynomial_isolates_ket_000103
      (bc := bc) (w := w) hvac (hWspan hwW) hw0 with
  ⟨n, p, c, hc, hpc⟩
have hketn : bc.ket n ∈ W := by
  have hpoly : (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) w ∈ W := by
    exact Polynomial.aeval_apply_smul_mem_of_le_comap hwW p bc.number hnum
  rw [hpc] at hpoly
  have hunit : c⁻¹ • (c • bc.ket n) ∈ W := Submodule.smul_mem W c⁻¹ hpoly
  simpa [smul_smul, hc] using hunit
have hvacW : bc.vacuum ∈ W := by
  have hdown : ∀ m : ℕ, bc.ket m ∈ W → bc.vacuum ∈ W := by
    intro m
    induction m with
    | zero =>
        intro hm
        simpa [bc.ket_zero] using hm
    | succ m ihm =>
        intro hm
        have hstep : bc.a (bc.ket (m + 1)) ∈ W := by
          simpa [Nat.succ_eq_add_one] using hWa (bc.ket (Nat.succ m)) hm
        have hscaled : ((↑(m + 1) : ℂ) • bc.ket m) ∈ W := by
          simpa [bc.a_ket_succ] using hstep
        have hcoeff : ((↑(m + 1) : ℂ)) ≠ 0 := by
          exact_mod_cast Nat.succ_ne_zero m
        have hm' : bc.ket m ∈ W := by
          have : ((↑(m + 1) : ℂ)⁻¹) • (((↑(m + 1) : ℂ) • bc.ket m)) ∈ W :=
            Submodule.smul_mem W ((↑(m + 1) : ℂ)⁻¹) hscaled
          simpa [smul_smul, hcoeff] using this
        exact ihm hm'
  exact hdown n hketn
have hspan_le : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≤ W :=
  AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_fock_span_le_of_vacuum_creation_stable_000039
    (bc := bc) W hvacW hWaD
exact le_antisymm hWspan hspan_le

op_000086.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, then there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` such that `Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)` for every `n : ℕ`, and for every `l : ℕ →₀ ℂ`, `Φ ⟨bc.number (Φ.symm l), by exact Submodule.mul_mem _ _ (Φ.symm l).property⟩ = fun n => ((n : ℂ) * l n)`.
op_000086.formalized_statement: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → ∃ Φ : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ), (∀ n : ℕ, Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)) ∧ ∀ l : ℕ →₀ ℂ, Φ ⟨bc.number (Φ.symm l), (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_fock_span_invariant_under_ladder_000010 (bc := bc) (v := bc.a (Φ.symm l)) ((AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_fock_span_invariant_under_ladder_000010 (bc := bc) (v := ((Φ.symm l : Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)) : V)) (Φ.symm l).property).1)).2⟩ = l.sum (fun n c => Finsupp.single n ((n : ℂ) * c))
op_000086.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the diagonal action on Finsupp as a sum of singles, since a raw lambda is not directly a Finsupp.

op_000086.proof_sketch: This target has a strong proof direction from existing derived results. op_000098.source_statement: For every complex vector space V and bosonic core bc : BosonCore V with bc.vacuum ≠ 0, let S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), and let A : Module.End ℂ S be the restriction of bc.a to S. Then for every v : S, if A v = 0, there exists a unique scalar c : ℂ such that v = c • ⟨bc.vacuum, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self 0)⟩.
op_000098.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∀ v : S, bc.a v = 0 → ∃! c : ℂ, v = c • ⟨bc.ket 0, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self 0)⟩
op_000098.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the restricted-operator condition as `bc.a v = 0` for `v : S`, and used `bc.ket 0` as the canonical vacuum element of `S`.

op_000098.proof_sketch: This looks like a standard kernel-of-lowering-operator statement on the cyclic span of the vacuum, and the existing theorem `thm_ket_span_linear_equiv_finsupp_000067` is the likely key input. Under that linear equivalence, elements of `S = span (range bc.ket)` can be written in Fock coordinates, with `bc.ket 0` corresponding to the basis vector at `0`; the hypothesis `bc.a v = 0` should translate to the usual recurrence forcing all coefficients in degrees `> 0` to vanish. The ladder formula `a_ket_succ` gives `bc.a (bc.ket (n+1)) = (n+1) • bc.ket n`, so in characteristic zero over `ℂ` the only vector killed by `a` in this span is a scalar multiple of `ket 0`. Existence is therefore immediate, and uniqueness follows from `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` together with `ket_zero`, since if `c • ket 0 = d • ket 0` then `(c - d) • bc.vacuum = 0`, hence `c = d`. So the target has a promising proof direction rather than a counterexample direction.

op_000098.source_statement: For every complex vector space V and bosonic core bc : BosonCore V with bc.vacuum ≠ 0, let S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), and let A : Module.End ℂ S be the restriction of bc.a to S. op_000098.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∀ (A : Module.End ℂ S), (∀ s : S, (A s : V) = bc.a (s : V)) → ∀ v : S, A v = 0 → ∃! c : ℂ, v = c • ⟨bc.vacuum, by simpa [S, bc.ket_zero] using (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self 0) : bc.ket 0 ∈ S)⟩

op_000098.proof_sketch: This target has a clear proof direction. Use `thm_ket_span_linear_equiv_finsupp_000067`: for `S = Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` and `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, there is a linear equivalence `Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` sending `ket n` to `Finsupp.single n 1`, so every `v : S` has unique Fock coordinates. From the hypothesis on `A`, `A v = 0` implies `(bc.a (v : V)) = 0`. Writing `v = ∑ n, c n • ket n`, the ladder relation `a_ket_succ` gives `bc.a v = ∑ n, c (n+1) • ((n+1 : ℂ) • ket n)`, so by linear independence of the kets (equivalently by the `Φ` coordinates) and the fact `(n+1 : ℂ) ≠ 0`, all coefficients above degree `0` vanish. Hence `v = c 0 • ket 0`, and `ket_zero` identifies this with the required scalar multiple of the vacuum element of `S`. Uniqueness follows from `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`: if `c • vacuumS = d • vacuumS`, then `(c - d) • bc.vacuum = 0`, so `c = d`.

op_000081.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V` be the linear map defined by `Phi p = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`. Then there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `(Polynomial ℂ ⧸ LinearMap.ker Phi) ≃ₗ[ℂ] Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`.
op_000081.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), let Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V := (LinearMap.applyₗ (R := ℂ) bc.vacuum).comp (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger).toLinearMap; Nonempty ((Polynomial ℂ ⧸ LinearMap.ker Phi) ≃ₗ[ℂ] Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket))
op_000081.statement_formalization_notes: Phi is formalized as polynomial evaluation at bc.aDagger followed by applying the resulting endomorphism to bc.vacuum.

op_000081.proof_sketch: Apply `LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange` to the polynomial state map `Phi`. Then prove `LinearMap.range Phi = Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`: monomials `X^n` map to `bc.ket n`, so the ket span is contained in the range, while `Polynomial.aeval_endomorphism` writes every `Phi p` as a finite linear combination of the `bc.ket n`, so the range is contained in the ket span.
op_000081.proof_text:
classical
let Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V :=
  (LinearMap.applyₗ (R := ℂ) bc.vacuum).comp (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger).toLinearMap
change Nonempty ((Polynomial ℂ ⧸ LinearMap.ker Phi) ≃ₗ[ℂ] Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket))
refine ⟨(LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange Phi).trans <| LinearEquiv.ofEq _ _ ?_⟩
apply le_antisymm
· rintro _ ⟨p, rfl⟩
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)
  rw [Polynomial.aeval_endomorphism, Polynomial.sum_def]
  refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
  intro n hn
  simpa [BosonCore.ket] using
    Submodule.smul_mem (Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)) (p.coeff n)
      (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n))
· refine Submodule.span_le.mpr ?_
  rintro _ ⟨n, rfl⟩
  refine ⟨Polynomial.X ^ n, ?_⟩
  simp [Phi, BosonCore.ket]

op_000103.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and vector `w : V`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, `w ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`, and `w ≠ 0`, then there exist a natural number `n`, a polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, and a scalar `c : ℂ` with `c ≠ 0` such that `(Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) w = c • bc.ket n`.
op_000103.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (w : V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → w ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) → w ≠ 0 → ∃ (n : ℕ) (p : Polynomial ℂ) (c : ℂ), c ≠ 0 ∧ (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) w = c • bc.ket n

op_000103.proof_sketch: Write `w = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum` using the ket-span characterization. Let `n = q.natDegree`, so `q.coeff n ≠ 0`. Use Lagrange interpolation on the finite set `q.support` to choose `p` with `p.eval n = 1` and `p.eval m = 0` for every other `m ∈ q.support`. Since each `bc.ket m` is a nonzero eigenvector of `bc.number` with eigenvalue `m`, `Polynomial.aeval bc.number p` acts on `bc.ket m` by the scalar `p.eval m`, killing all support terms except `n` and leaving `q.coeff n • bc.ket n`.
op_000103.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc w hvac hspan hw
rcases
    (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_mem_ket_span_iff_exists_aeval_000075 (bc := bc) (v := w)).mp hspan with
  ⟨q, rfl⟩
have hq : q ≠ 0 := by
  intro hq
  apply hw
  simp [hq]
let n : ℕ := q.natDegree
have hn : n ∈ q.support := by
  exact Polynomial.natDegree_mem_support_of_nonzero hq
have hc : q.coeff n ≠ 0 := by
  exact Polynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hn
let p : Polynomial ℂ :=
  Lagrange.interpolate q.support (fun m : ℕ => (m : ℂ))
    (fun m : ℕ => if m = n then (1 : ℂ) else 0)
have hli : LinearIndependent ℂ (fun m : ℕ => bc.ket m) :=
  AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006
    (bc := bc) hvac
have hket_eig : ∀ m : ℕ, bc.number.HasEigenvector (m : ℂ) (bc.ket m) := by
  intro m
  refine ⟨Module.End.mem_eigenspace_iff.mpr ?_, LinearIndependent.ne_zero m hli⟩
  simpa using AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_number_ket_eigenvalue_000001 (bc := bc) m
have hket_apply : ∀ m : ℕ, (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (bc.ket m) = p.eval (m : ℂ) • bc.ket m := by
  intro m
  simpa using
    Module.End.aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector
      (f := bc.number) (p := p) (μ := (m : ℂ)) (x := bc.ket m) (hket_eig m)
have hcast_inj : Set.InjOn (fun m : ℕ => (m : ℂ)) ↑q.support := by
  intro i hi j hj hij
  exact Nat.cast_injective hij
have hpn : p.eval (n : ℂ) = 1 := by
  dsimp [p]
  rw [Lagrange.eval_interpolate_at_node
    (s := q.support)
    (v := fun m : ℕ => (m : ℂ))
    (r := fun m : ℕ => if m = n then (1 : ℂ) else 0)
    hcast_inj hn]
  simp
refine ⟨n, p, q.coeff n, hc, ?_⟩
rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060 (bc := bc) q]
rw [Polynomial.sum_def, map_sum]
simp_rw [map_smul, hket_apply, smul_smul]
rw [Finset.sum_eq_single_of_mem n hn]
· simp [hpn]
· intro m hm hmn
  have hpm : p.eval (m : ℂ) = 0 := by
    dsimp [p]
    rw [Lagrange.eval_interpolate_at_node
      (s := q.support)
      (v := fun k : ℕ => (k : ℂ))
      (r := fun k : ℕ => if k = n then (1 : ℂ) else 0)
      hcast_inj hm]
    simp [hmn]
  simp [hpm, hmn]

op_000103.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (w : V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → w ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) → w ≠ 0 → ∃ (n : ℕ) (p : Polynomial ℂ) (c : ℂ), c ≠ 0 ∧ (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) w = c • bc.ket n
op_000103.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly with existing `BosonCore.number`, `BosonCore.ket`, `Submodule.span`, and `Polynomial.aeval` notation.

op_000103.proof_sketch: This looks provable, and the previous failure came from relying on missing custom theorem names rather than from a bad statement. Use Mathlib's `Finsupp.mem_span_range_iff_exists_finsupp` to write `w` as a finite sum `∑ m, a_m • bc.ket m`; since `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, `thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006` makes the ket family linearly independent, so `w ≠ 0` gives some index `n` with coefficient `a_n ≠ 0`. Then `thm_number_ket_eigenvalue_000001` shows each `bc.ket m` is an eigenvector of `bc.number` with eigenvalue `m`, and Mathlib's `aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector` implies `(Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (bc.ket m) = p.eval m • bc.ket m`. Choose `p` by `Lagrange.interpolate` on the finite support so that `p.eval n = 1` and `p.eval m = 0` for every other support index; applying `Polynomial.aeval bc.number p` to `w` leaves exactly `a_n • bc.ket n`, with `a_n ≠ 0`.

op_000093.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, there exists a `ℂ`-linear map `Φ : Polynomial ℂ → Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` such that `Φ 1` is `bc.vacuum` viewed in the ket span, `Φ (Polynomial.X ^ n)` is `bc.ket n` viewed in the ket span for every `n : ℕ`, and for every polynomial `p`, `Φ (Polynomial.X * p)` is the restricted creation operator applied to `Φ p` while `Φ (Polynomial.derivative p)` is the restricted annihilation operator applied to `Φ p`.
op_000093.formalized_statement: ∀ (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → ∃ Φ : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), (Φ 1 : V) = bc.vacuum ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, (Φ (Polynomial.X ^ n) : V) = bc.ket n) ∧ (∀ p : Polynomial ℂ, (Φ (Polynomial.X * p) : V) = bc.aDagger (Φ p : V)) ∧ (∀ p : Polynomial ℂ, (Φ (Polynomial.derivative p) : V) = bc.a (Φ p : V))
op_000093.statement_formalization_notes: Restricted ladder actions are formalized as equalities after coercing ket-span elements back to V.

op_000093.proof_sketch: Define the map by polynomial evaluation at `bc.aDagger` on the vacuum, then codrestrict its image to the ket span using the verified support-sum theorem. The `1`, `X^n`, and `X * p` identities are direct `aeval` computations. The derivative identity is proved by expanding `aeval` on the vacuum as a finite ket sum and applying `bc.a_ket_succ` termwise.
op_000093.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc _
classical
let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)
let Φ0 : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V :=
  (LinearMap.applyₗ (R := ℂ) bc.vacuum).comp (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger).toLinearMap
have hΦ0 : ∀ p : Polynomial ℂ, Φ0 p ∈ S := by
  intro p
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum ∈ S
  rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060 (bc := bc) p]
  refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
  intro n hn
  exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n))
let Φ : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] S := Φ0.codRestrict S hΦ0
refine ⟨Φ, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
· change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger 1) bc.vacuum = bc.vacuum
  simp
· intro n
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X ^ n)) bc.vacuum = bc.ket n
  simp [BosonCore.ket]
· intro p
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * p)) bc.vacuum =
    bc.aDagger ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum)
  simp
· have ha_aeval :
    ∀ p : Polynomial ℂ,
      bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) =
        (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p.derivative) bc.vacuum := by
    intro p
    rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060 (bc := bc) p,
      Polynomial.derivative_apply, map_sum, map_sum]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl ?_
    intro n hn
    cases n with
    | zero =>
        simp [bc.vacuum_annihilate, BosonCore.ket]
    | succ n =>
        simp [Nat.succ_eq_add_one, bc.a_ket_succ, BosonCore.ket, smul_smul,
          mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
  intro p
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) bc.vacuum =
    bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum)
  simpa using (ha_aeval p).symm

op_000026.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, natural number `N`, and coefficient function `c : ℕ → ℂ`, if `T = ∑ k in Finset.range (N + 1), (c k : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ k)`, then `bc.a * T - T * bc.a = ∑ k in Finset.range N, ((((k + 1 : ℕ) : ℂ) * c (k + 1)) : ℂ) • (bc.aDagger ^ k)`.

op_000027.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and endomorphism `T : Module.End ℂ V`, `bc.a * (bc.aDagger * T) - (bc.aDagger * T) * bc.a = T + bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T - T * bc.a)`.
op_000027.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (T : Module.End ℂ V), bc.a * (bc.aDagger * T) - (bc.aDagger * T) * bc.a = T + bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T - T * bc.a)
op_000027.statement_formalization_notes: Used the existing `BosonCore` and `Module.End ℂ V` notation from `Theory.lean` with explicit universal quantification.

op_000027.proof_sketch: Rewrite the left side by associativity so `ccr'` applies to `bc.a * bc.aDagger`, expand `(bc.aDagger * bc.a + 1) * T`, regroup the additive terms, and finish with `mul_sub`.
op_000027.proof_text:
calc
  bc.a * (bc.aDagger * T) - (bc.aDagger * T) * bc.a
      = (bc.a * bc.aDagger) * T - bc.aDagger * (T * bc.a) := by
          simp [mul_assoc]
  _ = (bc.aDagger * bc.a + 1) * T - bc.aDagger * (T * bc.a) := by
          rw [bc.ccr']
  _ = bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T) + T - bc.aDagger * (T * bc.a) := by
          simp [add_mul, mul_assoc]
  _ = T + (bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T) - bc.aDagger * (T * bc.a)) := by
          abel_nf
  _ = T + bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T - T * bc.a) := by
          rw [mul_sub]

op_000027.proof_sketch: Introduce the quantified variables first, then reassociate the products so the CCR form `bc.ccr'` applies to `bc.a * bc.aDagger`. Expand by distributivity, rearrange the additive terms, and finish with `mul_sub`.
op_000027.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc T
calc
  bc.a * (bc.aDagger * T) - (bc.aDagger * T) * bc.a
      = (bc.a * bc.aDagger) * T - bc.aDagger * (T * bc.a) := by
          simp [mul_assoc]
  _ = (bc.aDagger * bc.a + 1) * T - bc.aDagger * (T * bc.a) := by
          rw [bc.ccr']
  _ = bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T) + T - bc.aDagger * (T * bc.a) := by
          simp [add_mul, mul_assoc]
  _ = T + (bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T) - bc.aDagger * (T * bc.a)) := by
          abel_nf
  _ = T + bc.aDagger * (bc.a * T - T * bc.a) := by
          rw [mul_sub]

op_000034.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) {v : V} {μ : ℂ} (n : ℕ), bc.number v = μ • v → (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0 → (bc.a ^ n) v ≠ 0 → μ = (n : ℂ)
op_000034.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) {v : V} {μ : ℂ} (n : ℕ), bc.number v = μ • v → (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0 → (bc.a ^ n) v ≠ 0 → μ = (n : ℂ)
op_000034.statement_formalization_notes: This is a valid Lean proposition in the repository's existing notation and aligns with the derived annihilation-eigenvalue shift theorem.

op_000034.proof_sketch: Set w := (bc.a ^ n) v. Then hnil gives bc.a w = 0, so bc.number w = 0. Applying the verified commutator formula for bc.a ^ n to v and using bc.number v = μ • v gives bc.number w = (μ - n) • w. Since w ≠ 0, scalar cancellation over ℂ forces μ - n = 0, hence μ = n.
op_000034.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc v μ n hμ hnil hnon
set w := (bc.a ^ n) v with hw
have hw_ne : w ≠ 0 := by
  simpa [hw] using hnon
have ha_w : bc.a w = 0 := by
  simpa [hw, pow_succ'] using hnil
have hw_num_zero : bc.number w = 0 := by
  unfold BosonCore.number
  rw [ha_w, map_zero]
have hcomm := congrArg (fun f => f v) (thm_comm_number_a_pow_000003 bc n)
have hcomm' : bc.number w - μ • w = (- (n : ℂ)) • w := by
  simpa [hw, hμ] using hcomm
have hw_num : bc.number w = (μ - (n : ℂ)) • w := by
  rw [sub_eq_iff_eq_add] at hcomm'
  simpa [sub_eq_add_neg, add_smul, add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc] using hcomm'
have hsmul : (μ - (n : ℂ)) • w = 0 := by
  calc
    (μ - (n : ℂ)) • w = bc.number w := by rw [hw_num]
    _ = 0 := hw_num_zero
have hsub : μ - (n : ℂ) = 0 := by
  exact (smul_eq_zero.mp hsmul).resolve_right hw_ne
exact sub_eq_zero.mp hsub

op_000034.proof_sketch: Use the verified theorem that repeated annihilation lowers the eigenvalue by n. Since `(bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0`, we get `bc.a ((bc.a ^ n) v) = 0`, hence the number operator kills `(bc.a ^ n) v`. Comparing this with the shifted-eigenvalue formula gives `(μ - n) • (bc.a ^ n v) = 0`, and the assumed nonvanishing of `(bc.a ^ n) v` forces `μ - n = 0`.
op_000034.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc v μ n hμ hnil hnon
have ha : bc.a ((bc.a ^ n) v) = 0 := by
  simpa [pow_succ'] using hnil
have hnum_zero : bc.number ((bc.a ^ n) v) = 0 := by
  unfold BosonCore.number
  change bc.aDagger (bc.a ((bc.a ^ n) v)) = 0
  rw [ha, map_zero]
have hdown :
    bc.number ((bc.a ^ n) v) = (μ - (n : ℂ)) • ((bc.a ^ n) v) := by
  simpa using thm_a_pow_shifts_eigenvalue_down_000008 (bc := bc) (v := v) (μ := μ) n hμ
have hsmul : (μ - (n : ℂ)) • ((bc.a ^ n) v) = 0 := by
  rw [← hdown]
  exact hnum_zero
have hsub : μ - (n : ℂ) = 0 := by
  exact (smul_eq_zero.mp hsmul).resolve_right hnon
exact sub_eq_zero.mp hsub

op_000037.source_statement: For every bosonic core `bc` and natural number `n`, `(bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket n) = ((n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum`.
op_000037.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (n : ℕ), (bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket n) = ((n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum
op_000037.statement_formalization_notes: Uses the existing `bc.ket` notation and explicit ambient vector-space binders from the repository.

op_000037.proof_sketch: Induct on n. The base case is `ket 0 = vacuum`. For the step, rewrite `a^(n+1)` as `(a^n) * a`, apply `a_ket_succ`, pull the scalar through `(bc.a ^ n)` by linearity, use the induction hypothesis, then collapse the nested scalar action with `smul_smul` and finish by `Nat.factorial_succ` plus `Nat.cast_mul`.
op_000037.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc n
induction n with
| zero =>
    simpa [BosonCore.ket]
| succ n ih =>
    calc
      (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket (n + 1))
          = ((bc.a ^ n) * bc.a) (bc.ket (n + 1)) := by
              rw [pow_succ]
      _ = (bc.a ^ n) (bc.a (bc.ket (n + 1))) := by
              rfl
      _ = (bc.a ^ n) (((n + 1 : ℂ)) • bc.ket n) := by
              rw [bc.a_ket_succ]
      _ = ((n + 1 : ℂ)) • (bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket n) := by
              rw [map_smul]
      _ = ((n + 1 : ℂ)) • ((((n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.vacuum) := by
              rw [ih]
      _ = (((n + 1 : ℂ) * (((n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ))) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum := by
              rw [smul_smul]
      _ = (((((n + 1).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ))) • bc.vacuum := by
              simpa [Nat.factorial_succ, Nat.cast_mul]

op_000037.proof_sketch: Induct on n. For the successor step, rewrite `(bc.a ^ (n+1))` as `(bc.a ^ n) * bc.a`, apply `bc.a_ket_succ`, pull the scalar through `(bc.a ^ n)` by linearity, use the induction hypothesis, and simplify the scalar with `smul_smul`, `Nat.cast_mul`, and `Nat.factorial_succ`.
op_000037.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc n
induction n with
| zero =>
    simp [BosonCore.ket]
| succ n ih =>
    calc
      (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket (n + 1))
          = (bc.a ^ n) (bc.a (bc.ket (n + 1))) := by
              rw [pow_succ]
              rfl
      _ = (bc.a ^ n) (((↑(n + 1) : ℂ)) • bc.ket n) := by
              rw [bc.a_ket_succ]
      _ = ((↑(n + 1) : ℂ)) • (bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket n) := by
              rw [map_smul]
      _ = ((↑(n + 1) : ℂ)) • ((((n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.vacuum) := by
              rw [ih]
      _ = ((((n + 1) * n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.vacuum := by
              rw [smul_smul, ← Nat.cast_mul]
      _ = (((n + 1).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum := by
              rw [Nat.factorial_succ]

op_000043.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (m n : ℕ), n < m → (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket n) = 0
op_000043.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (m n : ℕ), n < m → (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket n) = 0
op_000043.statement_formalization_notes: This directly reuses the existing `BosonCore.ket` notation from the theory context.

op_000043.proof_sketch: Prove first by induction on n that (a^(n+1)) kills ket n. The base case is the vacuum axiom, and the step applies a_ket_succ and linearity. Then from n < m write m = n + k + 1 and factor a^m as a^k composed with a^(n+1), so the stronger vanishing immediately gives the target.
op_000043.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc m n hnm
have hkill : ∀ n : ℕ, (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket n) = 0 := by
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      simpa [BosonCore.ket] using bc.vacuum_annihilate
  | succ n ih =>
      calc
        (bc.a ^ ((n + 1) + 1)) (bc.ket (n + 1))
            = (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.a (bc.ket (n + 1))) := by
                rw [pow_succ]
                rfl
        _ = (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) ((↑(n + 1) : ℂ) • bc.ket n) := by
                rw [bc.a_ket_succ]
        _ = (↑(n + 1) : ℂ) • (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket n) := by
                rw [map_smul]
        _ = 0 := by
                rw [ih, smul_zero]
obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_lt hnm
calc
  (bc.a ^ (n + k + 1)) (bc.ket n)
      = (bc.a ^ k) ((bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket n)) := by
          rw [show n + k + 1 = k + (n + 1) by omega, ← pow_add]
          rfl
  _ = 0 := by
      rw [hkill n, map_zero]

op_000043.proof_sketch: First prove by induction on n that `(bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket n) = 0`. The step uses `bc.a_ket_succ` and linearity of `bc.a ^ (n + 1)`. Then from `n < m`, write `m = n + k + 1`, reorder the exponent to `k + (n + 1)`, and use `pow_add` together with `Module.End.mul_apply` to factor `(bc.a ^ m)` as `(bc.a ^ k)` after the killing factor `(bc.a ^ (n + 1))`.
op_000043.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc m n hnm
have hkill : ∀ n : ℕ, (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket n) = 0 := by
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      simpa [BosonCore.ket] using bc.vacuum_annihilate
  | succ n ih =>
      calc
        (bc.a ^ ((n + 1) + 1)) (bc.ket (n + 1))
            = (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.a (bc.ket (n + 1))) := by
                rw [pow_succ]
                rfl
        _ = (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) ((↑(n + 1) : ℂ) • bc.ket n) := by
                rw [bc.a_ket_succ]
        _ = (↑(n + 1) : ℂ) • (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket n) := by
                rw [map_smul]
        _ = 0 := by
                rw [ih, smul_zero]
obtain ⟨k, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_lt hnm
calc
  (bc.a ^ (n + k + 1)) (bc.ket n)
      = (bc.a ^ (k + (n + 1))) (bc.ket n) := by
          rw [show n + k + 1 = k + (n + 1) by omega]
  _ = (bc.a ^ k) ((bc.a ^ (n + 1)) (bc.ket n)) := by
          simpa [Module.End.mul_apply] using
            congrArg (fun f => f (bc.ket n)) (pow_add bc.a k (n + 1))
  _ = 0 := by
          rw [hkill n, map_zero]

op_000083.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V`, if `bc.vacuum ∈ W`, `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, then there exists a unique bosonic core `bcW : BosonCore W` such that `(bcW.vacuum : V) = bc.vacuum`, `(bcW.a w : V) = bc.a (w : V)`, and `(bcW.aDagger w : V) = bc.aDagger (w : V)` for every `w : W`.

op_000083.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and `ℂ`-submodule `W ≤ V`, if `bc.vacuum ∈ W`, `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W`, and `∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W`, then there exists a unique bosonic core `bcW : BosonCore W` such that `(bcW.vacuum : V) = bc.vacuum`, `(bcW.a w : V) = bc.a (w : V)`, and `(bcW.aDagger w : V) = bc.aDagger (w : V)` for every `w : W`.

op_000083.proof_sketch: Use the existing existence theorem `thm_invariant_submodule_inherits_boson_core_000051` for the witness. For uniqueness, if another inherited core has the same ambient vacuum, annihilation, and creation maps after coercion to `V`, then its vacuum agrees in `W` by `Subtype.ext`, and its operators agree by `LinearMap.ext` plus `Subtype.ext`. After destructing the two `BosonCore` structures, the remaining proof fields are equal by proof irrelevance (`Subsingleton.elim`).
op_000083.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc W hvac ha haDagger
rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_invariant_submodule_inherits_boson_core_000051
    (bc := bc) (W := W) hvac ha haDagger with
  ⟨bcW, hVac, hA, hADagger⟩
refine ⟨bcW, ?_, ?_⟩
· exact ⟨hVac, hA, hADagger⟩
· intro bcW'
  rintro ⟨hVac', hA', hADagger'⟩
  have hVacW : bcW'.vacuum = bcW.vacuum := by
    apply Subtype.ext
    calc
      (bcW'.vacuum : V) = bc.vacuum := hVac'
      _ = (bcW.vacuum : V) := hVac.symm
  have hAW : bcW'.a = bcW.a := by
    ext w
    apply Subtype.ext
    calc
      (bcW'.a w : V) = bc.a (w : V) := hA' w
      _ = (bcW.a w : V) := (hA w).symm
  have hADaggerW : bcW'.aDagger = bcW.aDagger := by
    ext w
    apply Subtype.ext
    calc
      (bcW'.aDagger w : V) = bc.aDagger (w : V) := hADagger' w
      _ = (bcW.aDagger w : V) := (hADagger w).symm
  cases bcW with
  | mk a aDagger vacuum ccr vacuum_annihilate =>
      cases bcW' with
      | mk a' aDagger' vacuum' ccr' vacuum_annihilate' =>
          cases hAW
          cases hADaggerW
          cases hVacW
          have hccr : ccr' = ccr := Subsingleton.elim _ _
          have hvacuum_annihilate : vacuum_annihilate' = vacuum_annihilate :=
            Subsingleton.elim _ _
          cases hccr
          cases hvacuum_annihilate
          rfl

The operator fields agree by `ext w` and the given pointwise coercion equalities, noting that after extensionality the goal is already an equality in `V`, so no extra `Subtype.ext` is needed there. op_000083.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc W hvac ha haDagger
rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_invariant_submodule_inherits_boson_core_000051
    (bc := bc) (W := W) hvac ha haDagger with
  ⟨bcW, hVac, hA, hADagger⟩
refine ⟨bcW, ?_, ?_⟩
· exact ⟨hVac, hA, hADagger⟩
· intro bcW'

  have hVacW : bcW'.vacuum = bcW.vacuum := by

    ext w
    exact (hA' w).trans ((hA w).symm)
  have hADaggerW : bcW'.aDagger = bcW.aDagger := by

    exact (hADagger' w).trans ((hADagger w).symm)

  | mk a aDagger vacuum ccr vacuum_annihilate =>

op_000094.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, applying `bc.a` exactly `p.natDegree` times to `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum` yields `(((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff p.natDegree) • bc.vacuum`.
op_000094.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = ((((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff p.natDegree) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum
op_000094.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly using endomorphism powers and Polynomial.aeval at bc.aDagger.

op_000094.proof_sketch: Expand the polynomial state with `Polynomial.aeval_eq_sum_range`, prove by induction that `(bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket m) = (m! : ℂ) • bc.vacuum`, and separately that `(bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket i) = 0` for `i < m`. Then for `m = p.natDegree`, every lower term in the sum vanishes and only the top coefficient survives.
op_000094.proof_text:
let n := p.natDegree
have h_expand :
    (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum =
      ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), p.coeff i • bc.ket i := by
  rw [Polynomial.aeval_eq_sum_range]
  simp [BosonCore.ket, n]
have h_same : ∀ m : ℕ, (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket m) = ((m.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum := by
  intro m
  induction m with
  | zero => simp [BosonCore.ket]
  | succ m ih =>
      calc
        (bc.a ^ (m + 1)) (bc.ket (m + 1)) = (bc.a ^ m) (bc.a (bc.ket (m + 1))) := by
          simp [pow_succ]
        _ = (bc.a ^ m) ((↑(m + 1) : ℂ) • bc.ket m) := by rw [bc.a_ket_succ]
        _ = (↑(m + 1) : ℂ) • (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket m) := by simp
        _ = (↑(m + 1) : ℂ) • (((m.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum) := by rw [ih]
        _ = (((m + 1).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum := by
          rw [smul_smul, ← Nat.cast_mul, Nat.factorial_succ]
have h_lt : ∀ {m i : ℕ}, i < m → (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket i) = 0 := by
  intro m i hi
  induction m generalizing i with
  | zero => cases hi
  | succ m ih =>
      cases i with
      | zero =>
          simp [BosonCore.ket, bc.vacuum_annihilate, pow_succ]
      | succ i =>
          calc
            (bc.a ^ (m + 1)) (bc.ket (i + 1)) = (bc.a ^ m) (bc.a (bc.ket (i + 1))) := by
              simp [pow_succ]
            _ = (bc.a ^ m) ((↑(i + 1) : ℂ) • bc.ket i) := by rw [bc.a_ket_succ]
            _ = (↑(i + 1) : ℂ) • (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket i) := by simp
            _ = 0 := by
              have hsmall : i < m := by omega
              have hz : (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket i) = 0 := ih hsmall
              simp [hz]
have hsum0 : ∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, p.coeff i • (bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket i) = 0 := by
  apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
  intro i hi
  have hi' : i < n := Finset.mem_range.mp hi
  simp [h_lt hi']
calc
  (bc.a ^ n) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum)
      = (bc.a ^ n) (∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), p.coeff i • bc.ket i) := by rw [h_expand]
  _ = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), p.coeff i • (bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket i) := by
    simp [map_sum]
  _ = (∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, p.coeff i • (bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket i)) +
        p.coeff n • (bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket n) := by
    rw [Finset.sum_range_succ]
  _ = p.coeff n • (bc.a ^ n) (bc.ket n) := by rw [hsum0, zero_add]
  _ = p.coeff n • (((n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum) := by rw [h_same]
  _ = ((((n.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff n) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum := by
    rw [smul_smul, mul_comm]
  _ = ((((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff p.natDegree) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum := by
    simp [n]

op_000094.proof_sketch: Introduce the quantified variables first, then rewrite the vacuum state using the existing support-sum theorem for `Polynomial.aeval`. Push `(bc.a ^ p.natDegree)` through the finite sum by linearity, use `Finset.sum_eq_single` to isolate the `natDegree` term, kill every other support term via `thm_a_pow_ket_eq_zero_of_lt_000043`, and evaluate the surviving top term with `thm_a_pow_ket_self_factorial_000037`.
op_000094.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc p
by_cases hp : p = 0
· subst p
  simp
· calc
    (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum)
        = (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (p.sum (fun n z => z • bc.ket n)) := by
            rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060 (bc := bc) (p := p)]
    _ = (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (∑ i ∈ p.support, p.coeff i • bc.ket i) := by
            rw [Polynomial.sum_def]
    _ = ∑ i ∈ p.support, (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (p.coeff i • bc.ket i) := by
            rw [map_sum]
    _ = ∑ i ∈ p.support, p.coeff i • (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (bc.ket i) := by
            refine Finset.sum_congr rfl ?_
            intro i hi
            rw [map_smul]
    _ = p.coeff p.natDegree • (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (bc.ket p.natDegree) := by
            refine Finset.sum_eq_single p.natDegree ?_ ?_
            · intro i hi hne
              rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_a_pow_ket_eq_zero_of_lt_000043
                (bc := bc) (m := p.natDegree) (n := i) ?_, smul_zero]
              exact lt_of_le_of_ne (Polynomial.le_natDegree_of_mem_supp i hi) hne
            · intro hmem
              exact (hmem (Polynomial.natDegree_mem_support_of_nonzero hp)).elim
    _ = p.coeff p.natDegree • ((((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.vacuum) := by
            rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_a_pow_ket_self_factorial_000037
              (bc := bc) (n := p.natDegree)]
    _ = ((((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff p.natDegree) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum := by
            rw [smul_smul, mul_comm]

op_000094.proof_sketch: Expand the polynomial vacuum state as a finite support sum, push `(bc.a ^ p.natDegree)` through the sum by linearity, isolate the unique top-degree term in `Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1)`, kill all lower terms with `thm_a_pow_ket_eq_zero_of_lt_000043`, and evaluate the surviving top ket with `thm_a_pow_ket_self_factorial_000037`.
op_000094.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc p
rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060 (bc := bc) p,
  Polynomial.sum_over_range (p := p) (f := fun n z => z • bc.ket n) (by
    intro n
    simp)]
have hsum :
    (∑ a ∈ Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1), p.coeff a • (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (bc.ket a)) =
      p.coeff p.natDegree • (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (bc.ket p.natDegree) := by
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_single_of_mem p.natDegree (Finset.mem_range.mpr (Nat.lt_succ_self _))]
  intro n hn hne
  have hlt : n < p.natDegree := by
    exact lt_of_le_of_ne (Nat.le_of_lt_succ (Finset.mem_range.mp hn)) hne
  rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_a_pow_ket_eq_zero_of_lt_000043 (bc := bc) (m := p.natDegree) (n := n) hlt, smul_zero]
calc
  (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (∑ a ∈ Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1), p.coeff a • bc.ket a)
      = ∑ a ∈ Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1), p.coeff a • (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (bc.ket a) := by
          simp_rw [map_sum, map_smul]
  _ = p.coeff p.natDegree • (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (bc.ket p.natDegree) := hsum
  _ = p.coeff p.natDegree • ((((p.natDegree).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum) := by
          rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_a_pow_ket_self_factorial_000037]
  _ = (p.coeff p.natDegree * (((p.natDegree).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.vacuum := by
          simpa [smul_smul]
  _ = ((((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff p.natDegree) : ℂ) • bc.vacuum := by
          rw [mul_comm]

op_000101.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, every nonzero `ℂ`-submodule `W` of `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` that is invariant under the restricted annihilation operator contains the vacuum vector.
op_000101.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (W : Submodule ℂ V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) → W ≠ ⊥ → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W) → bc.vacuum ∈ W
op_000101.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the restricted-annihilation invariance conservatively as ambient invariance under `bc.a` together with `W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`.

op_000101.proof_sketch: A promising route is degree descent inside the ket span. First, `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` and `a_ket_succ` imply every `bc.ket n` is nonzero: if some `ket (n+1)` were zero, applying `bc.a` would force `ket n = 0`, eventually contradicting `ket_zero`. Next, `thm_number_ket_eigenvalue_000001` shows `bc.ket n` is a `bc.number`-eigenvector with eigenvalue `n`, so over `ℂ` the ket family has distinct eigenvalues and should be linearly independent by a standard Mathlib eigenvector-independence fact. Pick `0 ≠ w ∈ W`, write it uniquely as a finite linear combination of kets, let `N` be the largest index with nonzero coefficient, and apply `bc.a^[N]`; all lower-index terms vanish, while the top term becomes a nonzero scalar multiple of `bc.vacuum` by repeated use of `a_ket_succ`. Since `W` is `a`-invariant, that scalar multiple lies in `W`, and over `ℂ` this gives `bc.vacuum ∈ W`.

op_000102.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Φ : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V` be defined by `Φ(p) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`. Then `LinearMap.ker Φ` is an ideal of `Polynomial ℂ` that is stable under `Polynomial.derivative`; equivalently, if `Φ(p) = 0`, then `Φ(q * p) = 0` for every polynomial `q`, and `Φ(Polynomial.derivative p) = 0`.
op_000102.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum = 0 → (∀ q : Polynomial ℂ, (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (q * p)) bc.vacuum = 0) ∧ (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) bc.vacuum = 0
op_000102.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the equivalent kernel-closure statement explicitly, avoiding coercions from `LinearMap.ker` to an ideal.

op_000102.proof_sketch: The first conjunct is straightforward: `Polynomial.aeval` is an algebra hom, so `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (q * p)) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) * (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p)`. Applying both sides to `bc.vacuum`, the hypothesis `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum = 0` immediately gives `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (q * p)) bc.vacuum = 0`. For the derivative conjunct, the standard CCR pattern should apply: from `[bc.a, bc.aDagger] = 1`, one proves by polynomial induction the commutator identity `bc.a * Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p - Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p * bc.a = Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)`. Evaluating this at `bc.vacuum` and using `bc.vacuum_annihilate : bc.a bc.vacuum = 0` yields `bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) bc.vacuum`. Hence the hypothesis forces the derivative term to be `0` as well. So this target has a clear proof direction and matches a standard Mathlib/CCR commutator-with-polynomial pattern.

op_000102.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Φ : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V` be defined by `Φ(p) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`. Then `LinearMap.ker Φ` is an ideal of `Polynomial ℂ` that is stable under `Polynomial.derivative`; equivalently, if `Φ(p) = 0`, then `Φ(q * p) = 0` for every polynomial `q`, and `Φ(Polynomial.derivative p) = 0`.
op_000102.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), let Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V := (LinearMap.applyₗ (R := ℂ) bc.vacuum).comp (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger).toLinearMap; (∀ p q : Polynomial ℂ, Phi p = 0 → Phi (q * p) = 0) ∧ (∀ p : Polynomial ℂ, Phi p = 0 → Phi (Polynomial.derivative p) = 0)

op_000102.proof_sketch: Unfold Phi. For ideal closure, use `aeval_mul` so `Phi (q * p)` is `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q)` applied to `Phi p`, hence zero when `Phi p = 0`. For derivative stability, reuse the standard vacuum derivative identity proved by polynomial induction: `bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative q)) bc.vacuum`. Applying `bc.a` to the hypothesis `Phi p = 0` gives the derivative claim.

intro V _ _ bc

 constructor
 · intro p q hp
   change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (q * p)) bc.vacuum = 0
   rw [aeval_mul]
   simp [hp]
 · intro p hp
   have hderiv :
       ∀ q : Polynomial ℂ,
         bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) =
           (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative q)) bc.vacuum := by

               Polynomial.derivative_monomial_succ,

op_000102.proof_sketch: Introduce the local map `Phi` exactly as in the statement. For the multiplication clause, unfold `Phi` and use `Polynomial.aeval_mul`. For the derivative clause, use the same polynomial-induction identity appearing inside `thm_annihilated_polynomial_state_is_constant_000122`: `bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative q)) bc.vacuum`. Applying `congrArg bc.a` to the hypothesis then gives the desired vanishing of the derivative state.
op_000102.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc
let Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V :=
  (LinearMap.applyₗ (R := ℂ) bc.vacuum).comp (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger).toLinearMap
change
  (∀ p q : Polynomial ℂ, Phi p = 0 → Phi (q * p) = 0) ∧
    (∀ p : Polynomial ℂ, Phi p = 0 → Phi (Polynomial.derivative p) = 0)
constructor
· intro p q hp
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum = 0 at hp
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (q * p)) bc.vacuum = 0
  rw [Polynomial.aeval_mul]
  simp [hp]
· intro p hp
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum = 0 at hp
  have hderiv :
      ∀ q : Polynomial ℂ,
        bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) =
          (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative q)) bc.vacuum := by
    intro q
    induction q using Polynomial.induction_on' with
    | add q r hq hr =>
        simp [Polynomial.derivative_add, hq, hr]
    | monomial n c =>
        cases n with
        | zero =>
            simp [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060,
              bc.vacuum_annihilate]
        | succ n =>
            rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060,
              Polynomial.derivative_monomial_succ,
              AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060]
            simp [bc.a_ket_succ, smul_smul, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
  change (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) bc.vacuum = 0
  simpa [hderiv p] using congrArg bc.a hp
