op_000104.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` and `p ≠ 0`, then there exist a natural number `m` and a scalar `c : ℂ` with `c ≠ 0` such that `(bc.a ^ m) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = c • bc.vacuum`.
op_000104.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → p ≠ 0 → ∃ (m : ℕ) (c : ℂ), c ≠ 0 ∧ (bc.a ^ m) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = c • bc.vacuum
op_000104.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly with existing `BosonCore`, `Polynomial.aeval`, operator-power, and scalar-action notation.

op_000104.proof_sketch: Choose m = p.natDegree and c = ((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff p.natDegree. The equality is exactly thm_annihilation_power_extracts_leading_coeff_000094, and c is nonzero because natDegree lies in the support of a nonzero polynomial and factorial casts to a nonzero complex number. The vacuum nonzero hypothesis is not needed.
op_000104.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc p _ hp
refine ⟨p.natDegree, (((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ) * p.coeff p.natDegree), ?_, ?_⟩
· have hfact : (((p.natDegree.factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) ≠ 0 := by
    exact Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (Nat.factorial_ne_zero p.natDegree)
  have hcoeff : p.coeff p.natDegree ≠ 0 := by
    exact Polynomial.mem_support_iff.mp (Polynomial.natDegree_mem_support_of_nonzero hp)
  exact mul_ne_zero hfact hcoeff
· simpa using thm_annihilation_power_extracts_leading_coeff_000094 (bc := bc) (p := p)

op_000110.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V` be defined by `Phi(p) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`, and let `Q := Polynomial ℂ ⧸ LinearMap.ker Phi`. Then there exist `ℂ`-linear endomorphisms `A, A† : Module.End ℂ Q`, induced respectively by `Polynomial.derivative` and multiplication by `Polynomial.X`, such that `A * A† - A† * A = 1`, `A` annihilates the class of `1`, and there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Ψ : Q ≃ₗ[ℂ] Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` that intertwines `A` and `A†` with the restrictions of `bc.a` and `bc.aDagger` to `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`.
op_000110.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), let Φ : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V := (LinearMap.applyₗ (R := ℂ) (M := V) (M₂ := V) bc.vacuum).comp ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger).toLinearMap); let Q := Polynomial ℂ ⧸ LinearMap.ker Φ; let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∃ (A Adag : Module.End ℂ Q) (hSa : ∀ x : V, x ∈ S → bc.a x ∈ S) (hSad : ∀ x : V, x ∈ S → bc.aDagger x ∈ S) (Ψ : Q ≃ₗ[ℂ] S), A.comp (LinearMap.ker Φ).mkQ = (LinearMap.ker Φ).mkQ.comp BosonCore.polyDeriv ∧ Adag.comp (LinearMap.ker Φ).mkQ = (LinearMap.ker Φ).mkQ.comp BosonCore.polyMulX ∧ A * Adag - Adag * A = (1 : Module.End ℂ Q) ∧ A ((LinearMap.ker Φ).mkQ (1 : Polynomial ℂ)) = (0 : Q) ∧ Ψ.toLinearMap.comp A = (LinearMap.restrict bc.a hSa).comp Ψ.toLinearMap ∧ Ψ.toLinearMap.comp Adag = (LinearMap.restrict bc.aDagger hSad).comp Ψ.toLinearMap
op_000110.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized Φ explicitly via aeval and LinearMap.applyₗ, with quotient-induced operators encoded by mkQ commutation equalities.

op_000110.proof_sketch: This target has a standard quotient-by-kernel proof direction. Let `Φ` be the displayed polynomial-to-`V` map and `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`; by `aDagger_ket` and `a_ket_succ`, both `bc.aDagger` and `bc.a` preserve `S`, so the witnesses `hSad` and `hSa` are straightforward. The crucial identities are `Φ (BosonCore.polyMulX p) = bc.aDagger (Φ p)` and `Φ (BosonCore.polyDeriv p) = bc.a (Φ p)`; the second is checked on monomials using `a_ket_succ` and then extended by linearity. Therefore `ker Φ` is invariant under `polyMulX` and `polyDeriv`, so these induce endomorphisms `Adag, A : Module.End ℂ Q` with the required `mkQ` commutation formulas; surjectivity of `mkQ` then lets `BosonCore.poly_ccr` and `poly_vacuum` descend to `A * Adag - Adag * A = 1` and `A ((mkQ) 1) = 0`. For the equivalence, `range Φ = S` because every `Φ p` is a linear combination of kets and every ket is `Φ (X^n)`; `thm_mem_ket_span_iff_exists_aeval_000075` should package this image description cleanly. Then compose `LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange` with the identification `LinearMap.range Φ = S` to obtain `Ψ`, and the intertwining equations follow from the same two identities above.

op_000110.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, let `Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V` be defined by `Phi(p) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum`, and let `Q := Polynomial ℂ ⧸ LinearMap.ker Phi`. Then there exist `ℂ`-linear endomorphisms `A, A† : Module.End ℂ Q`, induced respectively by `Polynomial.derivative` and multiplication by `Polynomial.X`, such that `A * A† - A† * A = 1`, `A` annihilates the class of `1`, and there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Ψ : Q ≃ₗ[ℂ] Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` that intertwines `A` and `A†` with the restrictions of `bc.a` and `bc.aDagger` to `Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`.
op_000110.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), let Phi : Polynomial ℂ →ₗ[ℂ] V := (LinearMap.applyₗ (R := ℂ) bc.vacuum).comp (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger).toLinearMap; let Q := Polynomial ℂ ⧸ LinearMap.ker Phi; let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∃ (A ADagger : Module.End ℂ Q), A.comp (LinearMap.ker Phi).mkQ = (LinearMap.ker Phi).mkQ.comp BosonCore.polyDeriv ∧ ADagger.comp (LinearMap.ker Phi).mkQ = (LinearMap.ker Phi).mkQ.comp BosonCore.polyMulX ∧ A * ADagger - ADagger * A = 1 ∧ A ((LinearMap.ker Phi).mkQ (1 : Polynomial ℂ)) = 0 ∧ ∃ bcS : BosonCore S, (bcS.vacuum : V) = bc.vacuum ∧ (∀ s : S, (bcS.a s : V) = bc.a (s : V)) ∧ (∀ s : S, (bcS.aDagger s : V) = bc.aDagger (s : V)) ∧ ∃ Ψ : Q ≃ₗ[ℂ] S, Ψ.toLinearMap.comp A = bcS.a.comp Ψ.toLinearMap ∧ Ψ.toLinearMap.comp ADagger = bcS.aDagger.comp Ψ.toLinearMap
op_000110.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized induced quotient operators via commutation with `mkQ`, and encoded restrictions on the ket span using a bosonic core on that submodule.

op_000110.proof_sketch: The proof direction is still plausible. The repository already contains the main ingredients: `thm_aeval_vacuum_kernel_mul_derivative_stable_000102` for kernel stability under multiplication and derivative, `thm_invariant_submodule_inherits_boson_core_000051` for the induced boson core on `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`, and `thm_polynomial_quotient_equiv_ket_span_000081` for the quotient-to-ket-span equivalence. The remaining formalization gap is the exact Lean assembly of the quotient endomorphisms via `Submodule.mapQ` together with a canonical `Ψ := LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange Phi` transported along `range Phi = S`, and then proving the final intertwining equalities with `bcS.a` and `bcS.aDagger` in the precise bundled form required by the statement. I do not have a sufficiently reliable tactic script for those final `mapQ`/`quotKerEquivRange` compatibility steps without speculative lemma usage.

op_000112.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = l.sum (fun n c => (p.eval (n : ℂ) * c) • bc.ket n)
op_000112.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → ((Polynomial.aeval bc.number) p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = l.sum (fun n c => (p.eval (n : ℂ) * c) • bc.ket n)
op_000112.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the statement directly using the existing BosonCore number and ket definitions and Finsupp linearCombination.

op_000112.proof_sketch: Use linearity of `((Polynomial.aeval bc.number) p)` over `Finsupp.linearCombination`, then prove each `bc.ket n` is sent to `(p.eval (n : ℂ)) • bc.ket n` by combining the derived eigenvalue theorem for `bc.number` on kets with Mathlib's `aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector`. The hypothesis `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` is used only to obtain `bc.ket n ≠ 0` via linear independence of the ket family.
op_000112.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc l p hvac
calc
  ((Polynomial.aeval bc.number) p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l)
      = Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ
          (fun n : ℕ => ((Polynomial.aeval bc.number) p) (bc.ket n)) l := by
            rw [Finsupp.apply_linearCombination]
  _ = Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => (p.eval (n : ℂ)) • bc.ket n) l := by
        refine congrArg (fun v => Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ v l) ?_
        funext n
        have hket_ne : bc.ket n ≠ 0 := by
          exact
            LinearIndependent.ne_zero n
              (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006
                (bc := bc) hvac)
        have hEig : bc.number.HasEigenvector (n : ℂ) (bc.ket n) := by
          refine ⟨?_, hket_ne⟩
          rw [Module.End.mem_eigenspace_iff]
          simpa using
            AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_number_ket_eigenvalue_000001 (bc := bc) n
        simpa using
          (aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector (f := bc.number) (p := p) (μ := (n : ℂ))
            (x := bc.ket n) hEig)
  _ = l.sum (fun n c => (p.eval (n : ℂ) * c) • bc.ket n) := by
        rw [Finsupp.linearCombination_apply]
        simp [smul_smul, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]

op_000112.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = l.sum (fun n c => (p.eval (n : ℂ) * c) • bc.ket n)
op_000112.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) l) = l.sum (fun n c => (p.eval (n : ℂ) * c) • bc.ket n)
op_000112.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the given statement using existing BosonCore, Finsupp.linearCombination, and Polynomial.aeval notation.

op_000112.proof_sketch: This target has a standard diagonalization proof. First use linearity of `((Polynomial.aeval bc.number) p)` to push it through `Finsupp.linearCombination`, reducing to the action on each basis vector `bc.ket n`. For each `n`, reuse `thm_number_ket_eigenvalue_000001` to get `bc.number (bc.ket n) = (n : ℂ) • bc.ket n`, and combine it with `thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006` plus `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` to obtain `bc.ket n ≠ 0`, so `bc.ket n` is a `HasEigenvector` for eigenvalue `n`. Then Mathlib’s eigenvector polynomial-evaluation lemma (`aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector`) gives `((Polynomial.aeval bc.number) p) (bc.ket n) = (p.eval (n : ℂ)) • bc.ket n`, and the remaining step is the routine `Finsupp` rewrite turning the resulting linear combination into `l.sum (fun n c => (p.eval (n : ℂ) * c) • bc.ket n)`.

op_000119.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. Then there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` such that `Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)` for every `n : ℕ`, and for every polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, the image under `Φ` of the vector `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum` viewed in `S` is exactly `p.toFinsupp`.
op_000119.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∃ Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ), (∀ n : ℕ, Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)) ∧ ∀ p : Polynomial ℂ, ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum ∈ S) ∧ ∀ hp : (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum ∈ S, Φ ⟨(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum, hp⟩ = p.toFinsupp
op_000119.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the ket-span equivalence and encoded polynomial states in the span using explicit subtype membership witnesses.

op_000119.proof_sketch: Use `AutomatedTheoryConstruction.main_thm_fock_span_linear_equiv_polynomial` to get a linear equivalence `Ψ : Polynomial ℂ ≃ₗ[ℂ] S` sending each polynomial to its vacuum-created state in the ket span `S`. Then define `Φ := Ψ.symm.trans (Polynomial.toFinsuppIsoLinear ℂ)`. The ket basis clause comes from applying `Ψ` to `X^n`, and the polynomial-state clause follows by subtype extensionality together with the defining property of `Ψ`.
op_000119.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc hvac
dsimp
rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.main_thm_fock_span_linear_equiv_polynomial (bc := bc) hvac with
  ⟨Ψ, hΨ⟩
refine ⟨Ψ.symm.trans (Polynomial.toFinsuppIsoLinear ℂ), ?_⟩
constructor
· intro n
  have hket : (Ψ (Polynomial.X ^ n) : V) = bc.ket n := by
    simpa [BosonCore.ket] using hΨ (Polynomial.X ^ n)
  have hket' :
      (⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ :
        Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)) = Ψ (Polynomial.X ^ n) := by
    apply Subtype.ext
    simpa using hket.symm
  simpa [Polynomial.toFinsupp_X_pow] using
    congrArg (fun s => (Ψ.symm.trans (Polynomial.toFinsuppIsoLinear ℂ)) s) hket'
· intro p
  constructor
  · simpa [hΨ p] using (Ψ p).property
  · intro hp
    have hstate :
        (⟨(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum, hp⟩ :
          Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)) = Ψ p := by
      apply Subtype.ext
      simpa using (hΨ p).symm
    simpa using
      congrArg (fun s => (Ψ.symm.trans (Polynomial.toFinsuppIsoLinear ℂ)) s) hstate

op_000119.proof_sketch: Take the coordinate map on the ket span coming from linear independence of the ket family: `hli.linearCombinationEquiv.symm`. The ket basis clause is `repr_eq_single`. For polynomial states, use the existing span-membership theorem for vacuum-created states, then use the support-sum expansion of `aeval` on the vacuum to show that the coordinate vector is exactly `p.toFinsupp` via `hli.repr_eq`.
op_000119.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc hvac
dsimp
have hli : LinearIndependent ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) :=
  AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006
    (bc := bc) hvac
refine ⟨hli.linearCombinationEquiv.symm, ?_⟩
constructor
· intro n
  simpa [LinearIndependent.repr] using
    hli.repr_eq_single n
      ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ rfl
· intro p
  constructor
  · exact
      (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_mem_ket_span_iff_exists_aeval_000075
        (bc := bc) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum)).2 ⟨p, rfl⟩
  · intro hp
    have hp' :
        (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum =
          Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) p.toFinsupp := by
      simpa [Polynomial.sum_def, Finsupp.linearCombination_apply, Finsupp.sum,
        Polynomial.support_toFinsupp, Polynomial.toFinsupp_apply] using
        AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060
          (bc := bc) p
    simpa [LinearIndependent.repr] using
      (hli.repr_eq
        (x := ⟨(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum, hp⟩)
        (l := p.toFinsupp)
        (by simpa using hp'.symm))

op_000122.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, and polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` and `bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = 0`, then there exists `c : ℂ` such that `p = Polynomial.C c`.
op_000122.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = 0 → ∃ c : ℂ, p = Polynomial.C c
op_000122.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization using the existing `BosonCore` fields and `Polynomial.aeval` notation.

op_000122.proof_sketch: Prove first that annihilating a polynomial vacuum state corresponds to evaluating the derivative polynomial on the vacuum, by polynomial induction using the support-sum formula for `aeval` and the ladder relation `a_ket_succ`. Then the hypothesis gives `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) bc.vacuum = 0`. Injectivity of polynomial vacuum evaluation under `bc.vacuum ≠ 0` yields `Polynomial.derivative p = 0`, and Mathlib's `Polynomial.eq_C_of_derivative_eq_zero` shows that `p` is constant.
op_000122.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc p hvac ha
have hderiv :
    ∀ q : Polynomial ℂ,
      bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) =
        (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative q)) bc.vacuum := by
  intro q
  induction q using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add q r hq hr =>
      simp [Polynomial.derivative_add, hq, hr]
  | monomial n c =>
      cases n with
      | zero =>
          simp [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060,
            bc.vacuum_annihilate]
      | succ n =>
          rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060,
            Polynomial.derivative_monomial_succ,
            AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060]
          simp [bc.a_ket_succ, smul_smul, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
have hz : (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) bc.vacuum = 0 := by
  simpa [hderiv p] using ha
have hdp : Polynomial.derivative p = 0 := by
  apply
    (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_vacuum_injective_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000017
      (bc := bc) hvac)
  simpa using hz
exact ⟨p.coeff 0, Polynomial.eq_C_of_derivative_eq_zero hdp⟩

op_000123.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (n : ℕ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → n ∈ l.support → ∃ p : Polynomial ℂ, (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun m : ℕ => bc.ket m) l) = l n • bc.ket n
op_000123.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (l : ℕ →₀ ℂ) (n : ℕ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → n ∈ l.support → ∃ p : Polynomial ℂ, (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (Finsupp.linearCombination ℂ (fun m : ℕ => bc.ket m) l) = l n • bc.ket n
op_000123.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the given statement using existing BosonCore and Finsupp notation.

With those in place, a finite-support interpolation polynomial would finish the argument. As it stands, formalizing this directly would require first proving those intermediate lemmas.

op_000124.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (q : Polynomial ℂ) (n : ℕ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → n ∈ q.support → ∃ p : Polynomial ℂ, (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) = q.coeff n • bc.ket n
op_000124.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (q : Polynomial ℂ) (n : ℕ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → n ∈ q.support → ∃ p : Polynomial ℂ, (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) = q.coeff n • bc.ket n
op_000124.statement_formalization_notes: Used the repository's existing `BosonCore.number`, `bc.ket`, and `Polynomial.aeval` notation; this proposition elaborates successfully.

op_000124.proof_sketch: Choose a Lagrange interpolation polynomial on q.support that is 1 at n and 0 at every other support index. Using the derived theorem that each ket is a number-eigenvector, Mathlib's aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector shows p(number) acts diagonally on each ket. Expanding the polynomial state as the support sum over kets, all terms except n vanish.
op_000124.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc q n hvac hqn
classical
let p : Polynomial ℂ :=
  Lagrange.interpolate q.support (fun m : ℕ => (m : ℂ))
    (fun m => if m = n then (1 : ℂ) else 0)
have hcast_inj : Set.InjOn (fun m : ℕ => (m : ℂ)) q.support := by
  intro i hi j hj hij
  exact Nat.cast_injective hij
have heval : ∀ m ∈ q.support, p.eval (m : ℂ) = if m = n then 1 else 0 := by
  intro m hm
  simp [p, Lagrange.eval_interpolate_at_node, hcast_inj, hm]
have hli : LinearIndependent ℂ (fun m : ℕ => bc.ket m) :=
  AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006
    (bc := bc) hvac
have hket_ne : ∀ m : ℕ, bc.ket m ≠ 0 := by
  intro m
  exact hli.ne_zero m
have heig : ∀ m : ℕ, bc.number.HasEigenvector (m : ℂ) (bc.ket m) := by
  intro m
  refine ⟨?_, hket_ne m⟩
  rw [Module.End.mem_eigenspace_iff]
  simpa using
    AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_number_ket_eigenvalue_000001 (bc := bc) m
have hdiag : ∀ r : Polynomial ℂ, ∀ m : ℕ,
    (Polynomial.aeval bc.number r) (bc.ket m) = r.eval (m : ℂ) • bc.ket m := by
  intro r m
  simpa using (Module.End.aeval_apply_of_hasEigenvector (p := r) (h := heig m))
refine ⟨p, ?_⟩
calc
  (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum)
      = (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (q.sum (fun m z => z • bc.ket m)) := by
          rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060
            (bc := bc) q]
  _ = (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (∑ m in q.support, q.coeff m • bc.ket m) := by
        rw [Polynomial.sum_def]
  _ = ∑ m in q.support, q.coeff m • (Polynomial.aeval bc.number p) (bc.ket m) := by
        simp [map_sum]
  _ = ∑ m in q.support, (q.coeff m * p.eval (m : ℂ)) • bc.ket m := by
        refine Finset.sum_congr rfl ?_
        intro m hm
        rw [hdiag p m]
        simp [smul_smul, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
  _ = ∑ m in q.support, (if m = n then q.coeff m else 0) • bc.ket m := by
        refine Finset.sum_congr rfl ?_
        intro m hm
        rw [heval m hm]
        split_ifs <;> simp [mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
  _ = q.coeff n • bc.ket n := by
        rw [Finset.sum_eq_single_of_mem n hqn]
        intro m hm hmn
        simp [hmn]

op_000132.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, vector `v : V`, and scalar `μ : ℂ`, if `bc.number v = μ • v`, `v ≠ 0`, and there exists a natural number `m` such that `(bc.a ^ m) v = 0`, then there exists a unique natural number `n` such that `μ = (n : ℂ)`, `(bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0`, and `(bc.a ^ n) v ≠ 0`.
op_000132.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (v : V) (μ : ℂ), bc.number v = μ • v → v ≠ 0 → (∃ m : ℕ, (bc.a ^ m) v = 0) → ∃! n : ℕ, μ = (n : ℂ) ∧ (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0 ∧ (bc.a ^ n) v ≠ 0
op_000132.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the claim with explicit universal quantifiers and a unique existential over the level n.

op_000132.proof_sketch: Take the least k such that (bc.a ^ k) v = 0. Since v ≠ 0, one has k > 0, so write k = n + 1 with n = Nat.pred k. Minimality gives (bc.a ^ n) v ≠ 0, and the verified theorem `thm_eigenvalue_from_annihilation_length_000034` then forces μ = n. Uniqueness follows from the equality μ = (n : ℂ) and injectivity of Nat.cast into ℂ.
op_000132.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc v μ hμ hv hnil
classical
let k : ℕ := Nat.find hnil
have hk : (bc.a ^ k) v = 0 := Nat.find_spec hnil
have hk_ne : k ≠ 0 := by
  intro hk0
  apply hv
  simpa [k, hk0] using hk
let n : ℕ := Nat.pred k
have hk_eq : k = n + 1 := by
  dsimp [n]
  simpa [Nat.succ_eq_add_one] using
    (Nat.succ_pred_eq_of_pos (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hk_ne)).symm
have hkill : (bc.a ^ (n + 1)) v = 0 := by
  simpa [hk_eq] using hk
have hprev : (bc.a ^ n) v ≠ 0 := by
  intro hnzero
  have hk_le : k ≤ n := Nat.find_min' hnil hnzero
  have : n + 1 ≤ n := by
    simpa [hk_eq] using hk_le
  exact Nat.not_succ_le_self n this
have hμn : μ = (n : ℂ) := by
  exact AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_eigenvalue_from_annihilation_length_000034
    (bc := bc) (v := v) (μ := μ) n hμ hkill hprev
refine ⟨n, ?_, ?_⟩
· exact ⟨hμn, hkill, hprev⟩
· intro n' hn'
  rcases hn' with ⟨hμ', _, _⟩
  apply Nat.cast_injective (R := ℂ)
  exact hμ'.symm.trans hμn

mt_manual.proof_sketch: Fix `bc` and `hvac`, and obtain `Φ` from `main_thm_fock_span_linear_equiv_polynomial bc hvac`. Rewrite all three target identities using the theorem’s pointwise formula for `Φ`. For `Φ 1 = vacuum`, simplify `Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger 1`. For the creation intertwining, rewrite `Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.X * p)` using `aeval_mul` and `aeval_X`; this gives exactly `bc.aDagger ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum)`. For annihilation, prove the helper lemma `bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)) bc.vacuum`; the clean route is to check it on monomials using the ladder action on Fock states, then sum over the polynomial support using `thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060`, and finally substitute back through the defining formula of `Φ`.

op_000133.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (m n : ℕ), (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket n) = if m ≤ n then (((n.factorial / (n - m).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.ket (n - m) else 0
op_000133.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (m n : ℕ), (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket n) = if m ≤ n then (((n.factorial / (n - m).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.ket (n - m) else (0 : V)
op_000133.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the stated closed formula, using the existing `BosonCore.ket` notation.

op_000133.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (m n : ℕ), (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket n) = if m ≤ n then (((n.factorial / (n - m).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.ket (n - m) else 0
op_000133.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (m n : ℕ), (bc.a ^ m) (bc.ket n) = if m ≤ n then (((n.factorial / (n - m).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ)) • bc.ket (n - m) else 0
op_000133.statement_formalization_notes: This matches the given statement directly and uses the repository's existing `BosonCore`, `ket`, and power notation.

op_000133.proof_sketch: Induct on m. The base case is simp. In the step, split on m.succ ≤ n. In the lowering case, apply the induction hypothesis, then use `a_ket_succ` on `ket (n - m)`, and convert the scalar with `Nat.descFactorial_eq_div` plus `Nat.descFactorial_succ`. In the vanishing case, the induction hypothesis already gives zero, so one more application of `a` stays zero.
op_000133.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc m
induction m generalizing n with
| zero =>
    intro n
    simp
| succ m ih =>
    intro n
    by_cases hmn : m.succ ≤ n
    · have hm : m ≤ n := Nat.le_trans (Nat.le_succ m) hmn
      have hsub : n - m = (n - m.succ) + 1 := by
        omega
      have hcoef : (n.factorial / (n - m).factorial) * (n - m) =
          n.factorial / (n - m.succ).factorial := by
        rw [← Nat.descFactorial_eq_div hm, ← Nat.descFactorial_eq_div hmn,
          Nat.descFactorial_succ, Nat.mul_comm]
      rw [pow_succ', ih n, if_pos hm, map_smul, hsub, bc.a_ket_succ, if_pos hmn, smul_smul]
      have hsub' : (n - m.succ) + 1 = n - m := by
        omega
      congr 1
      rw [hsub']
      exact_mod_cast hcoef
    · have hmnot : ¬ m ≤ n := by
        omega
      rw [pow_succ', ih n, if_neg hmnot]
      simp [if_neg hmn]

op_000135.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. Then for every natural number `m` and every `s : S`, one has `((bc.a ^ m) (s : V) = 0)` if and only if `s` lies in the `ℂ`-span of the vectors `bc.ket n` with `n < m`.
op_000135.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∀ (m : ℕ) (s : S), (bc.a ^ m) (s : V) = 0 ↔ (s : V) ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range fun n : Fin m => bc.ket (n : ℕ))
op_000135.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the vectors with n < m as Set.range over Fin m to avoid set-comprehension ambiguity.

Those ingredients are not yet available as reusable lemmas, and reconstructing them locally would require substantial new intermediate infrastructure rather than a direct formalization of the supplied sketch.

op_000136.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. If `W` is a nonzero `ℂ`-submodule of `S` that is invariant under the restrictions of `bc.a` and `bc.aDagger`, then `W = S`.
op_000136.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (W : Submodule ℂ V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket) → W ≠ ⊥ → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.a v ∈ W) → (∀ v : V, v ∈ W → bc.aDagger v ∈ W) → W = Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)
op_000136.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the submodule of S conservatively as an ambient submodule W of V with W ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket), matching existing repository practice for restricted-operator invariance.

op_000078.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.a * Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p - Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p * bc.a = Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)
op_000078.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ), bc.a * Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p - Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p * bc.a = Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative p)
op_000078.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly as the operator commutator identity for polynomial evaluation at `aDagger`.

op_000078.proof_sketch: This target is a standard CCR-to-polynomial-commutator statement: from `bc.ccr`, one first proves the power case `[bc.a, bc.aDagger^(n+1)] = (n+1) • bc.aDagger^n`, which is essentially already available as `a_mul_aDagger_pow_succ`. Then expand `Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p` termwise using the usual `aeval`/linearity lemmas, so the commutator with `bc.a` reduces to the sum of the commutators of each monomial. The constant term vanishes because scalar multiples of `1` commute with every endomorphism, and the monomial term matches the derivative coefficient `(n+1)` exactly. So the promising Lean route is polynomial induction or `p` expanded into monomials, reusing `a_mul_aDagger_pow_succ` together with standard Mathlib facts about `Polynomial.aeval`, `Polynomial.derivative`, and linearity.

op_000140.proof_sketch: Express v as a polynomial state on the vacuum, take m = p.natDegree for the nonzero polynomial p, expand over the ket basis, note that a^m kills every lower ket and sends ket m to m! times the vacuum, and use the nonzero top coefficient.
op_000140.proof_text:
intro hvac hvspan hv
rcases (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_mem_ket_span_iff_exists_aeval_000075 (bc := bc) v).mp hvspan with ⟨p, rfl⟩
have hp : p ≠ 0 := by
  intro hp0
  apply hv

op_000140.proof_sketch: Write the nonzero vector in the ket span as a polynomial in the creation operator applied to the vacuum. Take the polynomial's top degree m. Then a^m kills every lower ket term, sends ket m to m! times the vacuum, and the top coefficient times m! is nonzero.

rcases (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_mem_ket_span_iff_exists_aeval_000075 (bc := bc) v).mp hvspan with ⟨p, rfl⟩

refine ⟨p.natDegree, p.coeff p.natDegree * (((p.natDegree).factorial : ℕ) : ℂ), mul_ne_zero hcoeff hfact, ?_⟩
rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060 (bc := bc) p,
  Polynomial.sum_over_range (p := p) (f := fun n z => z • bc.ket n) (by intro n; simp)]
have hsum :
    (∑ a ∈ Finset.range (p.natDegree + 1), p.coeff a • (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (bc.ket a)) =
      p.coeff p.natDegree • (bc.a ^ p.natDegree) (bc.ket p.natDegree) := by

op_000147.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)`. Then there exists a `ℂ`-linear equivalence `Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)` such that `Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)` for every `n : ℕ`, and under this equivalence the induced operators of `bc.aDagger` and `bc.a` on `S` are exactly the standard creation and annihilation operators on finitely supported sequences: `A†(l) = l.sum (fun n c => Finsupp.single (n + 1) c)` and `A(l) = l.sum (fun n c => if n = 0 then 0 else Finsupp.single (n - 1) (((n : ℂ) * c)))`.
op_000147.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket); ∃ (Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)) (ADagger A : Module.End ℂ S), (∀ s : S, ((ADagger s : S) : V) = bc.aDagger (s : V)) ∧ (∀ s : S, ((A s : S) : V) = bc.a (s : V)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)) ∧ (∀ l : ℕ →₀ ℂ, Φ (ADagger (Φ.symm l)) = l.sum (fun n c => Finsupp.single (n + 1) c)) ∧ (∀ l : ℕ →₀ ℂ, Φ (A (Φ.symm l)) = l.sum (fun n c => if n = 0 then 0 else Finsupp.single (n - 1) ((n : ℂ) * c)))
op_000147.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the induced operators on the ket span by existentially quantifying restricted endomorphisms, since no canonical restriction name is present in Theory.lean.

op_000147.proof_sketch: Use the canonical ket-span/Finsupp equivalence coming from linear independence of the ket family. Define the standard creation and annihilation operators on `ℕ →₀ ℂ` by `Finsupp.lsum`, transport them back along `Φ`, and verify on the `Finsupp.single n c` basis that the transported operators agree with `bc.aDagger` and `bc.a` on the ket basis. Then evaluate the transported operators on arbitrary `l` by construction.
op_000147.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc hvac
 dsimp
 let S : Submodule ℂ V := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)
 change
   ∃ (Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)) (ADagger A : Module.End ℂ S),
     (∀ s : S, ((ADagger s : S) : V) = bc.aDagger (s : V)) ∧
       (∀ s : S, ((A s : S) : V) = bc.a (s : V)) ∧
         (∀ n : ℕ,
           Φ ⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩ =
             Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)) ∧
           (∀ l : ℕ →₀ ℂ,
             Φ (ADagger (Φ.symm l)) = l.sum (fun n c => Finsupp.single (n + 1) c)) ∧
             ∀ l : ℕ →₀ ℂ,
               Φ (A (Φ.symm l)) =
                 l.sum
                   (fun n c => if n = 0 then 0 else Finsupp.single (n - 1) ((n : ℂ) * c))
 let ketS : ℕ → S := fun n =>
   ⟨bc.ket n, by
     simpa [S] using
       (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n) :
         bc.ket n ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket))⟩
 have hli : LinearIndependent ℂ (fun n : ℕ => bc.ket n) :=
   AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_ket_linear_independent_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000006
     (bc := bc) hvac
 let Φ : S ≃ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ) := hli.linearCombinationEquiv.symm
 have hΦket : ∀ n : ℕ, Φ (ketS n) = Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ) := by
   intro n
   simpa [Φ, ketS, LinearIndependent.repr] using hli.repr_eq_single n (ketS n) rfl
 have hΦsymm_single_one : ∀ n : ℕ, Φ.symm (Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)) = ketS n := by
   intro n
   apply Φ.injective
   simpa [hΦket n]
 have hΦsymm_single : ∀ n : ℕ, ∀ c : ℂ, Φ.symm (Finsupp.single n c) = c • ketS n := by
   intro n c
   calc
     Φ.symm (Finsupp.single n c) = Φ.symm (c • Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)) := by simp
     _ = c • Φ.symm (Finsupp.single n (1 : ℂ)) := by simp
     _ = c • ketS n := by rw [hΦsymm_single_one n]
 let SDagger : Module.End ℂ (ℕ →₀ ℂ) :=
   Finsupp.lsum ℂ (fun n => Finsupp.lsingle (R := ℂ) (M := ℂ) (n + 1))
 let SA : Module.End ℂ (ℕ →₀ ℂ) :=
   Finsupp.lsum ℂ fun n =>
     if n = 0 then 0 else (n : ℂ) • Finsupp.lsingle (R := ℂ) (M := ℂ) (n - 1)
 let ADagger : Module.End ℂ S :=
   (Φ.symm : (ℕ →₀ ℂ) →ₗ[ℂ] S).comp (SDagger.comp (Φ : S →ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)))
 let A : Module.End ℂ S :=
   (Φ.symm : (ℕ →₀ ℂ) →ₗ[ℂ] S).comp (SA.comp (Φ : S →ₗ[ℂ] (ℕ →₀ ℂ)))
 have hADagger_map :
     (((Submodule.subtype S).comp ADagger).comp (Φ.symm : (ℕ →₀ ℂ) →ₗ[ℂ] S)) =
       (bc.aDagger.comp ((Submodule.subtype S).comp (Φ.symm : (ℕ →₀ ℂ) →ₗ[ℂ] S))) := by
   ext n c
   rw [Finsupp.lsingle_apply]
   simp [ADagger, SDagger, hΦsymm_single, ketS, bc.aDagger_ket, map_smul]
 have hA_map :
     (((Submodule.subtype S).comp A).comp (Φ.symm : (ℕ →₀ ℂ) →ₗ[ℂ] S)) =
       (bc.a.comp ((Submodule.subtype S).comp (Φ.symm : (ℕ →₀ ℂ) →ₗ[ℂ] S))) := by
   ext n c
   rw [Finsupp.lsingle_apply]
   cases n with
   | zero =>
       simp [A, SA, hΦsymm_single, ketS, bc.ket_zero, bc.vacuum_annihilate, map_smul]
   | succ n =>
       simp [A, SA, hΦsymm_single, ketS, bc.a_ket_succ, map_smul, smul_smul,
         mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
 have hADagger_eq :
     (Submodule.subtype S).comp ADagger = bc.aDagger.comp (Submodule.subtype S) := by
   ext s
   have hs := LinearMap.congr_fun hADagger_map (Φ s)
   simpa using hs
 have hA_eq : (Submodule.subtype S).comp A = bc.a.comp (Submodule.subtype S) := by
   ext s
   have hs := LinearMap.congr_fun hA_map (Φ s)
   simpa using hs
 refine ⟨Φ, ADagger, A, ?_⟩
 constructor
 · intro s
   simpa using LinearMap.congr_fun hADagger_eq s
 constructor
 · intro s
   simpa using LinearMap.congr_fun hA_eq s
 constructor
 · intro n
   simpa [S, ketS] using hΦket n
 constructor
 · intro l
   simpa [ADagger, SDagger]
 · intro l
   simpa [A, SA]

op_000148.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V` and bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V` with `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, let `S := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range bc.ket)` and let `N_S : Module.End ℂ S` be the endomorphism induced by `bc.number`. Then for every `λ : ℂ`, the eigenspace `LinearMap.eigenspace N_S λ` is zero unless `λ = (n : ℂ)` for some `n : ℕ`, and in that case `LinearMap.eigenspace N_S (n : ℂ)` is exactly the one-dimensional submodule spanned by `⟨bc.ket n, Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_range_self n)⟩`.
op_000148.statement_formalization_notes:
Formalized the induced operator existentially on the ket span to avoid committing to a specific restriction API name.

op_000149.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, and natural number `m`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, then `(bc.a ^ m) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = 0` if and only if every `n ∈ p.support` satisfies `n < m`.
op_000149.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ) (m : ℕ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → ((bc.a ^ m) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = 0 ↔ ∀ n ∈ p.support, n < m)
op_000149.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly with polynomial support membership over natural exponents.

op_000149.proof_sketch: Reduce repeated annihilation on a polynomial vacuum state to repeated polynomial differentiation, then use injectivity of vacuum evaluation under `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`. The remaining polynomial fact is exactly that `derivative^[m] p = 0` iff every exponent in `p.support` is `< m`, proved via `iterate_derivative_eq_zero` in one direction and `coeff_iterate_derivative` plus nonvanishing descending factorials in the other.
op_000149.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc p m hvac
have hderiv :
    ∀ q : Polynomial ℂ,
      bc.a ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) =
        (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative q)) bc.vacuum := by
  intro q
  induction q using Polynomial.induction_on' with
  | add q r hq hr =>
      simp [Polynomial.derivative_add, hq, hr]
  | monomial n c =>
      cases n with
      | zero =>
          simp [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060,
            bc.vacuum_annihilate]
      | succ n =>
          rw [AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060,
            Polynomial.derivative_monomial_succ,
            AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_a_dagger_vacuum_support_sum_000060]
          simp [bc.a_ket_succ, smul_smul, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
have hiter :
    ∀ k : ℕ, ∀ q : Polynomial ℂ,
      (bc.a ^ k) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum) =
        (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative^[k] q)) bc.vacuum := by
  intro k
  induction k with
  | zero =>
      intro q
      simp
  | succ k ih =>
      intro q
      rw [pow_succ']
      change bc.a ((bc.a ^ k) ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger q) bc.vacuum)) =
        (Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger (Polynomial.derivative^[k + 1] q)) bc.vacuum
      rw [ih, hderiv]
      rw [Function.iterate_succ_apply']
have hpoly :
    Polynomial.derivative^[m] p = 0 ↔ ∀ n ∈ p.support, n < m := by
  constructor
  · intro hz n hn
    by_contra hnm
    have hmn : m ≤ n := Nat.not_lt.mp hnm
    have hcoeff0 : (Polynomial.derivative^[m] p).coeff (n - m) = 0 := by
      simpa [hz]
    rw [Polynomial.coeff_iterate_derivative (k := m) p (n - m), Nat.sub_add_cancel hmn] at hcoeff0
    have hdesc : ((Nat.descFactorial n m : ℕ) : ℂ) ≠ 0 := by
      exact Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (by
        rw [Nat.descFactorial_eq_zero_iff_lt]
        exact hnm)
    have hpn : p.coeff n ≠ 0 := Polynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hn
    exact (smul_eq_zero.mp hcoeff0).elim hdesc hpn
  · intro hs
    by_cases hp : p = 0
    · simpa [hp]
    · exact Polynomial.iterate_derivative_eq_zero (hs _ (Polynomial.natDegree_mem_support_of_nonzero hp))
rw [hiter m p]
constructor
· intro hz
  have hz' : Polynomial.derivative^[m] p = 0 :=
    (AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_aeval_vacuum_injective_of_vacuum_ne_zero_000017
      (bc := bc) hvac) (by simpa using hz)
  exact hpoly.mp hz'
· intro hs
  have hz : Polynomial.derivative^[m] p = 0 := hpoly.mpr hs
  simpa [hz]

op_000150.source_statement: For every complex vector space `V`, bosonic core `bc : BosonCore V`, polynomial `p : Polynomial ℂ`, and scalar `μ : ℂ`, if `bc.vacuum ≠ 0`, `p ≠ 0`, and `bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = μ • ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum)`, then there exist `n : ℕ` and `c : ℂ` such that `c ≠ 0`, `μ = (n : ℂ)`, and `p = Polynomial.C c * Polynomial.X ^ n`.
op_000150.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (p : Polynomial ℂ) (μ : ℂ), bc.vacuum ≠ 0 → p ≠ 0 → bc.number ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) = μ • ((Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum) → ∃ (n : ℕ) (c : ℂ), c ≠ 0 ∧ μ = (n : ℂ) ∧ p = Polynomial.C c * Polynomial.X ^ n
op_000150.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly using the existing `BosonCore.number` and `Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger` notation.

op_000150.proof_sketch: This looks like a standard diagonalization argument in the Fock basis, so there is a plausible proof direction. First prove by induction that `bc.number (bc.ket n) = (n : C) • bc.ket n`, using `comm_number_aDagger`, `number_vacuum`, and `aDagger_ket`; this is the usual ladder-operator eigenvalue computation. Next expand `(Polynomial.aeval bc.aDagger p) bc.vacuum` as the finite sum of polynomial coefficients against the kets `bc.ket n`, since only powers of `aDagger` act on the vacuum. Then use the existing ket-basis result (`thm_ket_span_linear_equiv_finsupp_000067`, or equivalently the polynomial/Fock equivalence) to get linear independence of the `bc.ket n`. Comparing coefficients in `sum n, (n * a_n) |n> = mu * sum n, a_n |n>` forces `(n - mu) * a_n = 0` for every degree, so all nonzero coefficients occur in a single degree `n`, and necessarily `mu = n`. Since `p != 0`, that surviving coefficient `c` is nonzero, yielding `p = Polynomial.C c * Polynomial.X ^ n`.
